meta.tagline: One material-agnostic BIM core; materials and cost models plug in at the seams.
meta.baseUnit: Linear geometry stored as integer ticks @ 1/32in (1in=32 ticks). Area/volume/weight/price/cost derived as real. Money = real decimal USD.
meta.units: US customary (in, ft, lb, psi, psf, USD) over an integer-tick geometry substrate.
patterns: Structure-of-Arrays + zero-copy shared memory (Rust worker ↔ JS mirror)
Discriminated routing by stockForm, never by material
Open registry keys over closed enums for all material-specific vocabulary
meta.techStack: Rust/WASM worker owns canonical SoA tick arrays + dependency-graph recompute + BREP kernel (truck); JS main thread owns a read-only render mirror + ToolRunner; wasm-bindgen commands + shared linear memory (two channels); Neon/Postgres + Drizzle snapshots; Cloudflare Workers/R2 at the edge.
meta.pricing: stored PriceQuote snapshots (real decimal USD), region- and time-stamped; live supplier inventory is an integration, not a constant.
meta.consolidates: Supersedes the six prior artifacts (lumber, solver, drawing-workspace, design-standard, architecture, reference-library) into one cohesive schema.

aliasTable.supplier: served by materials-stock.SupplierSku catalog + cut-optimization buy-list

layers.purpose: The single canonical home for shared geometry: the tick base-unit vocabulary (tick, TickVec2, TickVec3, UnitVec3, quat) plus every cross-schema geometry primitive (Plane, Transform, BoundingBox, CoordinateFrame, Path2D, Segment) and the BREP push/pull kernel (Volume, Face, VolumeDelta, PushPullOp, the kernel mutation service). This is the lowest shared module — the Rust-resident floor (truck/BREP + the SoA tick store) that materials, solver, drawing, and design-standard all stand on. No type here carries material or domain meaning; this layer is material-agnostic by construction, which is exactly what lets new materials (CFS/concrete/masonry) reuse it unchanged. It exists to end three classes of collision at once: misplaced primitives reaching up into domain schemas (Plane in drawing, Transform/BoundingBox in materials, CoordinateFrame in drawing), the vec3-vs-vec3<int> typing drift (now resolved by named tick/unit vector types in BOTH 2D and 3D), and the ambiguous home of the BREP kernel that drawing already cross-refs as geometry-kernel@0.x.
layers.baseUnitCompliance: This layer DEFINES the invariant the rest of the schema obeys, and it now enforces it structurally rather than by convention. tick is the named integer 1/32in base unit; EVERY linear field in this layer is typed `tick` (or the tick-vector aliases), never bare i32/int — that is the whole reason `tick` is named, and the draft's leak of raw `i32` on TickVec3/BoundingBox/Volume.height/PushPullOp.distance is corrected to `tick` so single-sourcing the unit is real, not cosmetic. All linear/world-position geometry is stored as TickVec3 (vec3<tick>) — Plane.origin, Transform.origin, BoundingBox.min/max, Segment endpoints, Volume.height — and all plane-local 2D geometry as TickVec2 (vec2<tick>, the named 2D analogue that ends the same drift in Path2D vertices). Directions and normals are UnitVec3 (unit vec3, no length, never ticks) — Plane.normal, CoordinateFrame axes, Volume.extrudeAxis, Face.normal. Rotations are quat (unitless). Crucially, every DERIVED magnitude is real, never ticks^n: Face.area is f32 in2 (resolving the solver ticks^2 violation S2-D), Path2D.signedArea is f32 in2, Segment.length is f32 in, and Volume.volume / BoundingBox-derived size are f32 in3. There is no area/volume/weight/price type STORED here — those are derived reals computed on demand from tick geometry — so the 'never type area as ticks^2' rule is structurally enforced at its source.
layers.extensibilityNotes: This layer is the open-for-extension floor precisely because it is material-blind. New materials enter through the design-standard DesignStandard interface seam, not here — and they get the entire geometry vocabulary (planes, transforms, BREP volumes, cut planes, derived volume/area accessors) for free, with zero core edits: a cold-formed-steel section, a concrete formwork volume, and a wood stud all place via the same Transform, bound via the same BoundingBox, and cut along the same Plane. Cost/estimating extension is likewise unaffected: takeoff reads geometry (BoundingBox extents, Face.area for sheet goods/surfaces, Path2D.signedArea, Segment.length for linear runs, and Volume.volume for concrete/masonry/grout quantities) to compute quantities, but no price/cost type lives here, so adding supplier SKUs or cost models never touches the kernel. Volume.volume (derived real in3) was added precisely so the volumetric materials (concrete/masonry) the user wants next have a unit-clean quantity to take off — without it, volumetric takeoff would have had to re-derive solid volume downstream or misuse the AABB. The seams that keep this layer stable: (1) Plane.source is a discriminated provenance (named world plane | face-derived | UV-spanned) so future non-axis-aligned construction planes add a variant, not a field; (2) Face.inferredRole is an open enum hint (never a commitment) so new assembly roles (e.g. shearwall, slab) extend the hint set without changing Face's identity; (3) Volume stays geometry-only, so routing a concrete leaf to the volume output vs a wood leaf to the linear cut optimizer is a downstream decision, never a kernel edit. SketchPlane deliberately stays UP in the drawing layer (it wraps a Plane + U/V + source face) — only the bare Plane lives here, keeping the kernel free of workspace concerns.
resolvesFindings: S1-D (Plane single-homed in geometry-kernel)
S2-B (Transform/BoundingBox/CoordinateFrame relocated from domain schemas)
S2-D (Face.area and tributaryArea corrected from ticks^2 to derived real in2)
S3-A (dangling primitives Path2D, Segment defined as kernel types; VolumeDelta defined to kill the draft's own dangling ref)
S3-B (primitive vocabulary drift: named tick/TickVec2/TickVec3/UnitVec3/quat, and ALL linear fields typed `tick` not bare i32)
ambiguous-home (Volume/Face/PushPullOp promoted out of solver layer 1 to the kernel drawing already cross-refs)

types.desc: The base linear unit: a signed integer count of 1/32-inch increments. 1in = 32 ticks; 1ft = 384 ticks. Every world-space linear magnitude in the entire schema is stored in ticks to eliminate float drift on imperial fractions. This is a primitive alias over i32, not a struct; it exists to give the unit ONE named definition so the whole schema stops writing bare 'int'/'i32' and inconsistent 'ticks'/'tick'. Every linear field in this layer is typed `tick`, never raw i32 — that is what makes the single-source real rather than cosmetic.

fields.name: (value)
fields.note: Underlying signed 32-bit integer, 1/32in per count. Examples: 32=1in, 112=3.5in (dressed 2x4 face), 3072=96in, 3348=104.625in. Signed to allow relative offsets/deltas and inset (negative) push/pull.

types.notes: Conversion is exact: inches = ticks/32 (real). Reverse must round-to-nearest-tick at commit. Resolves the 'ticks vs tick vs int' vocabulary drift (S3-B). Derived magnitudes (area/volume/weight/price) are NEVER expressed in tick^n — they are real and live on the consuming/derived accessors, not here.
crossRefs: all four schemas (every linear field retypes to tick)

types.desc: A 2D point in a plane's local (u,v) coordinate system, stored as two integer tick coordinates (vec2<tick>). The 2D analogue of TickVec3 and the named home for plane-local sketch coordinates — added so Path2D vertices stop being bare 'vec2<int>' and the tick-vs-float drift is closed in 2D exactly as it is in 3D. A TickVec2 has no world position on its own; the Plane it belongs to lifts it to 3D via lift(u,v) = origin + u*basisU + v*basisV.

fields.name: u
fields.note: Coordinate along the host Plane's basisU.

fields.name: v
fields.note: Coordinate along the host Plane's basisV.

types.notes: Closes the 2D half of the vec drift (S3-B): solver/drawing sketch coordinates were untyped or bare vec2. Integer ticks (no float) so the lift to a TickVec3 world point is exact. Plane-local only — never a world position by itself.

types.desc: A position in 3D world space, stored as three integer tick coordinates (vec3<tick>). THE canonical type for any committed world point. Distinct from UnitVec3: a TickVec3 has magnitude and location; it is a place, not a direction.

fields.name: z
fields.note: Up axis by CoordinateFrame convention (Z=blue=up).

types.notes: Single named home for the tick-vector, ending the vec3<int>-vs-vec3 drift (S3-B). Now typed over `tick` (not raw i32) so the base unit is single-sourced. Replaces bare 'vec3' on committed world points across solver (Segment endpoints, Volume profile lift) and drawing (SnapTarget.point, Plane.origin, ReferenceAnchor.point, DrawOp.payload — all currently prose-promised to ticks). In the Rust SoA store these are three parallel i32 columns, not an interleaved struct.
crossRefs: workspace-render.SnapTarget.point (was bare vec3 — retype to TickVec3)
workspace-render.ReferenceAnchor.point (was bare vec3 — retype to TickVec3)

types.desc: A unitless direction or normal: a 3-component real vector constrained to unit length (|v|=1). Carries orientation only — no position, no magnitude, NEVER ticks. The deliberate counterpart to TickVec3 and the thing the analysis flags as missing: solver and drawing used bare 'vec3' for both positions and directions; this type names the direction half.
types.notes: Used for every normal/axis: Plane.normal, Plane.basisU/basisV, CoordinateFrame.{axisX,axisY,axisZ}, Volume.extrudeAxis, Face.normal, drawing.ReferenceAnchor.impliedDir, solver.Orientation.strongAxis/weakAxis. Real (float) is CORRECT here and is NOT a base-unit violation — a direction has no length to quantize. Invariant: must be normalized; recompute on edit.
crossRefs: materials-stock.ConnectionPoint.normal (was plain vec3 — retype to UnitVec3)

types.desc: A rotation as a unit quaternion (x,y,z,w). Unitless. The canonical rotation representation, replacing the 'quat | euler' ambiguity in materials.Transform with a single normalized form.

fields.name: w
fields.note: Scalar part. Identity = (0,0,0,1).

types.notes: Single rotation primitive consumed by Transform and (in drawing) NavigationState.orbit. Quaternion-only avoids gimbal/order ambiguity; an euler convenience may be a render-side adapter detail but is not stored here. RESOLVES the 'quat | euler' typing confirmed present in materials.Transform.rotation (source: rotation t:'quat | euler').

types.desc: An oriented infinite plane in 3D world space: an origin point plus an orthonormal in-plane basis (basisU, basisV) and outward normal. THE single shared planar primitive. A cut plane, a face plane, and the plane a SketchPlane wraps are all the same math object. Extracted here from drawing, where it was misplaced and reached into by materials and solver.

fields.name: origin
fields.note: A point on the plane, in world ticks. Was bare 'vec3' in drawing — now tick-typed.

fields.name: normal
fields.note: Outward unit normal (= basisU x basisV). Real direction, not ticks. Was 'vec3 (basis cross)' in drawing.

fields.name: basisU
fields.note: First in-plane spanning axis (unit). For a named world plane this is one of X/Y/Z (from drawing's basisAxes pair); for a face-derived plane it comes from the face. Replaces drawing's untyped basisAxes pair with two explicit unit axes.

fields.name: basisV
fields.note: Second in-plane spanning axis (unit), orthogonal to basisU. With basisU defines the (u,v) frame that TickVec2/Path2D/Segment live in.

fields.name: source
fields.note: Discriminated provenance of the basis — extension seam for future non-axis-aligned construction planes. Add a variant, not a field. Captures drawing's 'named by spanning axes OR face-derived' as a discriminator.

types.notes: RESOLVES S1-D (known collision #1): Plane was defined in drawing (group B, value) but consumed by materials.CutOperation.plane and solver.Face.plane. Now single-homed here as a value; all three reference it. drawing keeps SketchPlane, which WRAPS this Plane + local U/V + source face — only the bare Plane moved down. The 2D->3D lift lift(u,v)=origin + u*basisU + v*basisV is the shared projectToPlane primitive drawing's crossRefs already park 'low in geometry-kernel/util'. Note: drawing's original Plane carried a single 'basisAxes' pair; expanded to explicit basisU/basisV UnitVec3 so face-derived and uvSpanned planes (non-axis-aligned) are representable — the extension seam.
crossRefs: design-standard.SectionProperties (section is a Face profile on a Plane)

types.desc: Rigid placement in 3D world space: an origin (translation, in ticks) plus a rotation (quat). Decoupled from geometry so one shared mesh/spec serves thousands of instances (the flyweight contextual half). Pure geometry — no material content.

fields.name: origin
fields.note: Anchor point in model space, 1/32in ticks.

fields.name: rotation
fields.note: Orientation. Identity = (0,0,0,1). Collapsed from materials' 'quat | euler'.

fields.name: axisOrigin
fields.note: Which local feature is the origin (which corner/centerline anchors the stick). Placement convention, not extra geometry. Preserves geometry-kernel.Transform.axisOrigin (source default: bottom-center). [money = real decimal USD, single-sourced via PriceQuote — no cents]

types.notes: RESOLVES S2-B: was defined in materials, consumed by materials + solver.MemberPlacement.transform + design-standard (carry-over). Now single-homed here. This is the CONTEXTUAL per-instance half of the flyweight split (shared RenderMesh/Spec is intrinsic; per-instance Transform is contextual) — that split is correct, only the home was wrong. 'rotation: quat | euler' collapsed to quat-only (S3-B).

types.desc: An axis-aligned bounding box (AABB) in world ticks: min and max corners. Used for culling, snapping, broad-phase clash/clearance checks, and as a geometric basis for estimating extents. Pure geometry primitive.

fields.name: min
fields.note: Minimum corner (smallest x,y,z) in world ticks. Source json e.g. [0,0,0].

fields.name: max
fields.note: Maximum corner (largest x,y,z) in world ticks. Source json e.g. [48,112,3348].

types.notes: RESOLVES S2-B: was in materials (RenderMesh.boundingBox, derived from Dimensions+Transform), also carried by design-standard. Now single-homed here. Extents (max-min) are integer ticks; any DERIVED size/area/volume read off the box for takeoff is real (inches), computed on demand — never stored as ticks^2/ticks^3. NOTE: an AABB volume OVER-estimates a rotated/sloped solid; volumetric takeoff (concrete/masonry) must read Volume.volume, not the AABB — BoundingBox is for broad-phase and gross extents only.

types.desc: The global world datum: origin (0,0,0) plus the orthonormal X/Y/Z basis everything ultimately snaps to and resolves axis-locks against. A single shared, immutable, looked-up-once world reference (a singleton flyweight — one key, never copied per instance) — hence reference, not a per-instance value.

fields.name: origin
fields.note: World origin, conventionally (0,0,0).

fields.name: axisX
fields.note: X basis. Convention: X=red. (Expanded from drawing's bundled 'axes:{X,Y,Z}' into three explicit UnitVec3.)

fields.name: axisY
fields.note: Y basis. Convention: Y=green.

fields.name: axisZ
fields.note: Z basis. Convention: Z=blue=up.

types.notes: RESOLVES S2-B: was in drawing (group B) as a value object. Promoted here AND re-stereotyped reference (flyweight) — there is exactly ONE world frame (a singleton, looked up once), shared everywhere, never copied per instance. drawing's inference 'axis lock' resolves against this shared frame in 3D (against a SketchPlane's U/V in a locked view). The named world planes (Plane.source = XY/XZ/YZ) are derived from this frame's axis pairs.
crossRefs: drawing (was a value in group B)
Plane.source namedWorld (derived from these axis pairs)

types.desc: A straight line segment between two committed world points (two TickVec3 endpoints). The atomic 1D world primitive — a wall baseline, an edge, a path leg. Length is derived real, never stored.

fields.name: a
fields.note: Start point, world ticks.

fields.name: b
fields.note: End point, world ticks.

fields.name: length
fields.note: DERIVED real inches = |b-a|/32. Convenience accessor; not stored canonically (computed from endpoints) so it can never drift. The linear quantity estimating reads for per-run takeoff.

types.notes: RESOLVES dangling primitive (S3-A): used by solver.Wall.baseline ('Segment (plan)') but defined nowhere. Now a kernel primitive with tick endpoints; length is derived real (in), matching the area-is-real rule. NOTE: a plan baseline is conceptually 2D — represent it as a Segment whose endpoints share z (plan z), keeping one Segment type rather than forking a 2D variant.
crossRefs: Path2D (a profile is an ordered chain of plane-local edges; Segment is the world-space leg)

types.desc: An ordered list of 2D vertices in a plane's local (u,v) coordinate system, optionally closed, defining a polyline or polygon. The profile a Volume is extruded from and the boundary loop of a Face. 2D because it lives ON a Plane; the Plane's basisU/basisV lift it to 3D.

fields.name: vertices
fields.note: Ordered (u,v) points in plane-local ticks (named TickVec2, not bare vec2<int>). Integer ticks along basisU/basisV — no float drift.

fields.name: closed
fields.note: true = polygon (first/last implicitly joined); false = open polyline.

fields.name: signedArea
fields.note: DERIVED real in2 (shoelace), valid only when closed. Sign encodes winding (CCW=outward). NEVER ticks^2 — the canonical fix pattern for the solver area violation (S2-D).

types.notes: RESOLVES dangling primitive (S3-A): used by solver.Volume.profile ('Path2D — closed plan sketch') and solver.Face.boundary, defined nowhere. Now a kernel primitive. Coordinates are tick-quantized via TickVec2 (closing the 2D drift, S3-B); area is derived real — Face.area must follow (S2-D). Lift to 3D: world_pt = plane.origin + u*plane.basisU + v*plane.basisV.
crossRefs: workspace-render.DrawOp.payload (profile in world ticks, lifted via SketchPlane)

types.desc: An extruded BREP solid produced by pushing a closed 2D profile along an axis, then editable face-by-face via push/pull. The raw geometry a user manipulates. Geometry only — it does NOT know it is a wall; promotion to a building object is a separate, explicit step (solver.Wall). This is the 'dumb push/pull solid' the truck/BREP kernel owns.

fields.name: profile
fields.note: Closed plan sketch the solid was extruded from (plane-local ticks).

fields.name: basePlane
fields.note: The Plane the profile was sketched on. Makes the profile's (u,v) liftable to world without re-deriving — added so Volume is self-contained, not implicitly tied to a transient SketchPlane.

fields.name: extrudeAxis
fields.note: Extrusion direction (usually +Z = basePlane.normal). Direction, not position — correctly real/unit, not ticks. Source: 'vec3 (usually +Z)'.

fields.name: height
fields.note: Extrusion distance in ticks (typed `tick`, not raw i32).

fields.name: faces
fields.note: The bounding faces; push/pull acts on these.

fields.name: edits
fields.note: Ordered edit history (provenance of the current shape) — immutable PushPullOp value records.

fields.name: volume
fields.note: DERIVED real cubic inches of the actual solid (NOT the AABB). Added so volumetric materials (concrete/masonry/grout) have a unit-clean takeoff quantity. Computed from faces/profile on demand; never stored as ticks^3.

types.notes: RESOLVES ambiguous-home: lived in solver layer 1 but drawing's crossRefs already call it geometry-kernel@0.x ('workspace emits, kernel consumes'). Now explicitly homed here. Stays entity — it has identity (id) and a mutation history. Promotion to solver.Wall reads Volume but does not move it. Material-agnostic: a concrete formwork solid is also a Volume (the concrete leaf routes to volume output via Volume.volume, bypassing the linear cut optimizer — but the geometry is identical). Volume.volume added to serve forward requirement (2) estimating for forward requirement (1) volumetric materials.

types.desc: A bounded planar region of a Volume: a stable face index, a normal, the Plane it lies in, and a closed boundary loop. Push/pull acts on a face; its orientation lets the system INFER a likely building role (hint, never a commitment). An addressable VO — it has no independent lifecycle (it is owned by its Volume) but carries a stable index so it can be referenced by push/pull and by solver.Wall.sourceFace.

fields.name: index
fields.note: Stable index within the owning Volume.faces. This is the addressable handle 'Face ref' / building-placement.Wall.sourceFace resolve against — a Face is a VO with no global identity, so it is referenced by (Volume.id, index), not by a free-floating uuid. Fixes the draft's untyped 'Face ref'.

fields.name: normal
fields.note: Outward face normal (unit direction). Was bare 'vec3' in solver — retyped UnitVec3.

fields.name: plane
fields.note: The shared Plane this face lies in (origin+basis+normal). Single-sourced — same primitive as cut/sketch planes (S1-D).

fields.name: boundary
fields.note: Closed boundary loop in plane-local (u,v) ticks (TickVec2 vertices).

fields.name: area
fields.note: DERIVED real square inches (from boundary, in plane). RESOLVES S2-D: solver typed this 'ticks^2' — corrected to real in2, matching materials' 'not stored as tick^2' rule and materials-stock.SectionProperties' real in2. The quantity sheet-good/surface takeoff reads.

fields.name: inferredRole
fields.note: Open-enum HINT from normal+height (vertical normal -> likely wall; low horizontal -> floor). Never a commitment; user/solver can override. Open so new assembly roles (shearwall, slab) extend without changing Face.

types.notes: RESOLVES ambiguous-home + S2-D + S1-D in one type: promoted out of solver layer 1 to the kernel (drawing crossRefs SketchPlane.source -> 'geometry-kernel: Face'); area corrected ticks^2 -> real in2; plane is the shared Plane primitive. ADDED `index` to give the VO an addressable handle — the draft's 'Face ref'/'targetFace: Face ref'/solver.Wall.sourceFace had no defined referent, since a value object has no identity; (Volume.id, Face.index) is the well-formed reference. Face.area stays real so cost math never inherits a tick^2 unit error.

types.desc: The immutable record of the geometric change a push/pull applied to a Volume: which faces were added/removed/moved and the new derived metrics. Captured per edit so the change is replayable and the dirty-propagation engine can scope the recompute. Pure geometry diff — no material content.

fields.name: addedFaces
fields.note: Indices of faces created by the op (within the post-edit Volume.faces).

fields.name: removedFaces
fields.note: Indices of faces removed by the op (within the pre-edit Volume.faces).

fields.name: movedFaceDelta
fields.note: Signed tick displacement applied to the target face along its normal.

fields.name: dirtyRange
fields.note: World-tick extent touched by the edit — fed to the worker's dirty-propagation to scope recompute (zero-copy SoA span).

types.notes: DEFINES the type the draft left dangling (PushPullOp.result was typed 'VolumeDelta' with no card — a self-inflicted S3-A). Value (immutable) because it is a recorded diff, not a process. Its dirtyRange ties the BREP edit to the Rust worker's dependency-graph recompute over the SoA tick arrays.

types.desc: An immutable record of one push/pull edit: extrude or inset a target Face along its normal by a signed tick distance. Stored in Volume.edits as replayable provenance. This is the COMMAND/EVENT (a value), distinct from the kernel verb that applies it — which is the GeometryKernel service. (The draft stereotyped this 'service', but a service is a stateless verb; a thing stored in an edits[] history array as provenance is an immutable value/command record, not a process.)

fields.name: targetFaceVolume
fields.note: Volume.id of the face being edited (the entity half of the face reference).

fields.name: targetFaceIndex
fields.note: Face.index within that Volume. Together (targetFaceVolume, targetFaceIndex) is the well-formed face reference — replaces the draft's untyped 'Face ref'.

fields.name: distance
fields.note: Signed extrusion distance in ticks (typed `tick`; + extrude / - inset along normal).

fields.name: mode
fields.note: Direction semantics relative to the face normal.

fields.name: result
fields.note: The geometric change this op produced (now a DEFINED type) — the entry appended to Volume.edits.

types.notes: RESOLVES ambiguous-home: 'workspace emits (drawing.DrawOp pushPull), kernel consumes'. Re-stereotyped service -> value because it is STORED in Volume.edits[] as immutable history (a service is not persisted as data). The actual mutation verb is GeometryKernel.applyPushPull (the service below). targetFace split into (Volume.id, Face.index) to give the draft's 'Face ref' a defined referent. Applying a PushPullOp triggers dirty-propagation — the Rust worker's dependency-graph recompute over the SoA tick arrays.

types.desc: The kernel mutation verb: the Rust-resident BREP operations that apply a PushPullOp to a Volume (extrude/inset along a Face normal), produce a VolumeDelta, append it to Volume.edits, and signal the dirty range for recompute. This is the genuine «service» (a stateless process/verb over the SoA tick store) — separated out so PushPullOp can be the immutable command it is stored as.

fields.name: applyPushPull(volume, op)
fields.note: Apply a PushPullOp to a Volume; returns the geometric delta and appends the op to Volume.edits. Editing geometry invalidates downstream framing -> triggers a one-way re-solve (dirty-propagation).

fields.name: extrude(profile, basePlane, axis, height)
fields.note: Create a Volume from a closed Path2D profile pushed along an axis. The initial-creation verb behind drawing's SketchPlane lift -> kernel op.

fields.name: lift(plane, point2d)
fields.note: Project a plane-local TickVec2 to a world TickVec3 (origin + u*basisU + v*basisV). The shared projectToPlane primitive drawing parks 'low in the kernel'.

types.notes: Splits the draft's overloaded PushPullOp into VALUE (the recorded op) + SERVICE (this verb that applies it), fixing the DDD stereotype error: a process stored in an edits[] array cannot be a service. Service stereotype = verb/process, consistent with solver.CutOperation/PushPull semantics. Honors the dirty-propagation contract of the Rust worker (dependency-graph recompute over SoA tick arrays); exposes the shared lift/projectToPlane util drawing's crossRefs require.
crossRefs: Volume / Face / PushPullOp / VolumeDelta (operands and result)
architecture.RustWorker (kernel is Rust-resident, owns SoA tick store)

layers.purpose: Material-agnostic raw-stock data model, the cut/provenance chain, and the purchasable-SKU/price catalog for the framing pipeline. Owns WHAT a member is made of (Stock + its flyweight StockSpec), its parametric geometry (Dimensions), its pure section geometry (SectionProperties), the pieces and cuts that turn stock into real members (Piece, CutOperation, WasteRecord), self-weight that feeds the loads layer (Weight), the per-member fastener locations that feed the connection graph (ConnectionPoint), the material-blind takeoff measure the estimator rolls up (QuantityTakeoff), and the canonical purchasable-SKU + price-snapshot flyweights (SupplierSku, PriceQuote, PriceTier) the bottom-up estimate prices against. Carries NO mechanical design values and NO load/cost-MODEL computation — design values resolve through the DesignStandard seam; the load traversal and the estimate rollup are separate downstream concerns that CONSUME these types. This is the upstream data floor of the framing model; everything downstream references it, it references only the geometry-kernel and reference-library.
layers.baseUnitCompliance: This layer is the canonical tick-invariant reference implementation (registry baseUnitAudit verdict: COMPLIANT/exemplary), held to that standard verbatim. LINEAR geometry is integer ticks @ 1/32in: Dimensions.length/actualWidth/actualThickness, Piece.length, CutOperation.position/kerf, ConnectionPoint.position, StockSpec.allowableLengths, SupplierSku.stockLength, WasteRecord.kerfLength/offcutLength. DERIVED reals: Dimensions.crossSectionArea (in², explicitly NOT tick²), Dimensions.volume (in³), all SectionProperties (A in², S in³, I in⁴ — single-sources the fix for solver.Face.area:ticks², S2-D), all of Weight (lb/ft³, lb/ft, lb), all of QuantityTakeoff (lf/bf/ft²/ft³/ea — aggregate measures, never ticks²), and all money on PriceQuote/PriceTier (float USD — the one money representation, fixing solver's int-cents drift, S3-C). TickVec3 (vec3<int>, ticks) is used for ConnectionPoint.position (a committed world position); UnitVec3 (vec3, unitless) for ConnectionPoint.normal (a direction) — kept distinct, never conflated (S3-B). CutOperation.angle is deg (an angle, legitimately real). No area is ever typed ticks² anywhere in this layer.
layers.extensibilityNotes: New material types (cold-formed steel, concrete, masonry, hot-rolled steel) are added with ZERO edits to the core types here. Seams: (1) Stock.material + Stock.stockForm discriminators (MaterialClass + StockForm value types) route material identity and output path (linear→cut optimizer, sheet→nest, cast→formwork/volume, unit→count) without any per-material field on Stock; (2) StockSpec carries NO mechanical/design values — only an opaque designValueRef key the DesignStandard Strategy resolves to DesignValues per material; so a steel, concrete, and wood StockSpec are the SAME type with different discriminator+key values, and the leaf behind the seam (NDS_Wood / AISI_CFS / ACI_Concrete) owns the physics; (3) SectionProperties is public-domain geometry identical across materials (basis=gross here; effective/cracked/transformed computed by the strategy), so it needs no extension; new profiles extend via Dimensions.crossSectionShape and SectionProperties.axis, not new types. For ESTIMATING (forward-req 2): Piece carries a QuantityTakeoff (lf/bf/area/volume/count by stockForm) and CutOperation emits a WasteRecord — both material-blind measures the bottom-up estimator rolls up against the in-layer SupplierSku/PriceQuote/PriceTier catalog. Adding a new supplier or a new cost model means adding SupplierSku/PriceQuote rows (catalog data, flyweight lookup), never editing a type — the same open-for-extension property the material seam gives the physics side. PriceTier (break pricing) lets bulk/volume cost models plug in without a schema change.

types.desc: Discriminator value identifying what a Stock is made of. First half of the material seam: names the material family so the DesignStandard strategy routes to the correct leaf (NDS_Wood / AISI_CFS / AISC / ACI_Concrete / TMS). Carries no physics — physics lives behind the seam.

fields.name: material
fields.note: wood | cold-formed-steel | hot-rolled-steel | concrete | masonry. Open enum — adding a value is the ONLY core change to onboard a new material family (S2-C / forward-req 1). [open registry key, not closed enum — S1 material-extensibility]

fields.name: designStandardRef
fields.note: Opaque key into the DesignStandard catalog (NDS | AISI | AISC | ACI | TMS). Decouples material identity from the strategy implementation; never a mechanical value.

types.notes: New value type promoted from the inline 'material' discriminator on design-standard.Stock so the routing key is named and reusable. Single-sources material identity (S2-C).

types.desc: Discriminator value identifying the geometric form of raw stock, which decides the OUTPUT PATH of the pipeline (registry gotcha #3: concrete routes to formwork/volume, bypassing the linear cut optimizer). stockForm — not material — chooses cut vs nest vs formwork. Also the CANONICAL source of the takeoff dimension every QuantityTakeoff mirrors.

fields.name: form
fields.note: linear | sheet | cast | unit. linear→cut optimizer; sheet→nesting; cast→formwork/volume; unit→count (masonry/CMU). Drives which downstream solver consumes the Stock.

fields.name: takeoffBasis
fields.note: linearFeet | boardFeet | area | volume | count — the quantity dimension the estimator rolls up for this form (forward-req 2). Canonically determined by form; this is the explicit takeoff CONTRACT, and QuantityTakeoff.basis mirrors it (single-sourced here, not copied). [open key sourced from StockSpec; board-ft is wood-only, not core]

types.notes: New value type. Makes the 'stockForm decides output path' rule (design-standard.Stock note) a first-class, estimable contract instead of an inline enum (S2-C, forward-req 2).

types.desc: The immutable original member — a length/sheet/cast of raw stock, material-agnostic. Provenance root for any Piece cut from it. Holds identity, discriminators, geometry, and a flyweight spec pointer — but NO mechanical design values (those resolve through the DesignStandard seam) and NO inline price (that lives on SupplierSku→PriceQuote).

fields.name: id
fields.note: Stable identity; survives transforms and placement. Provenance root.

fields.name: material
fields.note: Material discriminator (S2-C). Routes to the DesignStandard leaf; replaces the bare 'material' enum on materials-stock.Stock.

fields.name: stockForm
fields.note: Form discriminator (S2-C). Decides cut vs nest vs formwork output path.

fields.name: specRef
fields.note: Pointer into the StockSpec flyweight catalog (was LumberSpec). Mechanicals are NOT stored here — they resolve via spec→DesignStandard (kills S1-A at the source).

fields.name: dimensions
fields.note: Parametric geometry (length is the free variable).

fields.name: supplierRefs
fields.note: Keys into the in-layer SupplierSku catalog; many suppliers per member. Feeds bottom-up cost (forward-req 2).

fields.name: gradeStamp
fields.note: As-printed mill/heat stamp, e.g. 'SPF · STUD · S-DRY · mill 0042' (wood) or a steel heat number. CONTEXTUAL per-instance data — correctly kept OUT of the StockSpec flyweight (intrinsic/contextual split, S1-B).

fields.name: placement

fields.name: revision
fields.note: Optimistic-lock / change tracking.

types.notes: Renamed LumberStock→Stock and merged with design-standard.Stock — same entity, single-sourced (S2-C). Strips all mechanical values (S1-A) and inline price (price lives on SupplierSku/PriceQuote in this layer, S3-C). gradeStamp stays per-instance (S1-B split).

types.desc: Shared flyweight every Stock points at: resolves material + designation + condition → an opaque design-value key plus density and allowable lengths. The exemplary flyweight (cited by every other schema as the pattern reference). Holds NO mechanical design values inline — only a designValueRef the DesignStandard strategy resolves — so a wood, steel, or concrete spec is the SAME type.

fields.name: key
fields.note: Opaque flyweight key, e.g. 'SPF-STUD-SDRY' (wood) or '362S162-33-50' (CFS). Looked up, never copied per instance.

fields.name: material
fields.note: Material discriminator (matches the owning Stock); the strategy uses this to pick the leaf.

fields.name: designation
fields.note: Material-neutral product designation. Wood: species+grade (e.g. 'SPF / Stud'). Steel: section call-out. Replaces the wood-only species+grade enums with one extensible field (S2-C).

fields.name: condition
fields.note: Service/processing condition: wood moisture (S-DRY|S-GRN|KD), steel coating/yield, concrete cure. Material-blind so the spec stays one type across materials (was LumberSpec.moistureCondition).

fields.name: treatment
fields.note: none | pressure-treated | fire-retardant | galvanized | epoxy-coated — extensible per material (was LumberSpec.treatment).

fields.name: designValueRef
fields.note: Opaque key the DesignStandard seam resolves to DesignValues (wood: Fb/Ft/Fc/FcPerp/Fv/E/Emin; steel: Fy/Fu/E; concrete: f'c). REPLACES the inline LumberSpec.mechanical→MechanicalProperties object — kills the triplicated wood design-value model (S1-A) and removes the intrinsic/contextual factor-stack violation (S1-B): no adjustment factors live here, they are contextual on placement.

fields.note: Self-weight density at stated condition (LumberSpec.density carried over). Feeds Weight; intrinsic to the spec.

fields.name: allowableLengths

The single biggest S1-A fix: removed the inline mechanical→MechanicalProperties + adjustmentFactors map; design values resolve ONCE through the seam (S1-A, S1-B). DesignValueKey/CitationKey/SpecKey are opaque key aliases (S3-A).

types.desc: Parametric geometry of a stock member: length is the free variable; cross-section is fixed by nominal size. The canonical tick-invariant reference — linear fields are integer ticks, area/volume are DERIVED reals (never tick²).

fields.name: nominalSize
fields.note: '2x4' (wood) / '600S162' (CFS) — naming only, not real dimensions.

fields.name: actualWidth
fields.note: Dressed face. 112 = 3.5in.

fields.name: actualThickness
fields.note: Dressed edge. 48 = 1.5in.

fields.name: crossSectionArea
fields.note: DERIVED real single-ply GROSS face area (label/quick-calc), explicitly NOT stored as tick² (counter-example to geometry-kernel.Face.area:ticks², S2-D): 5.25. The axis-aware, ply-multiplied structural section area is SectionProperties.area — distinct, see that type's notes to avoid drift.

fields.name: volume
fields.note: DERIVED: (length/32) × crossSectionArea. Feeds Weight and dead-load.

fields.name: crossSectionShape
fields.note: rectangular | I | C | HSS | round — extensible to non-wood profiles WITHOUT a new type (forward-req 1). [open profile key; exotic shapes added behind the leaf, not by core edit]

fields.name: edgeProfile
fields.note: eased | square — affects render bevel + nominal subtraction.

types.notes: Carried over verbatim as the tick-invariant reference implementation. crossSectionShape extended to non-rectangular profiles (forward-req 1). Demonstrates the base-unit invariant the whole schema must conform to (S2-D).

types.desc: Pure cross-section structural geometry: section area, section modulus, moment of inertia, about a chosen bending axis. Public-domain math identical across all materials. Single-homed HERE (was duplicated in design-standard). Carries NO E and NO design values — the strategy injects stiffness; effective/cracked/transformed sections are computed by the strategy, never owned here.

fields.name: area
fields.note: A = b·d·ply, the STRUCTURAL section area (axis-aware, ply-multiplied) — distinct from Dimensions.crossSectionArea (single-ply gross label). REAL in², not tick² — canonical fix for geometry-kernel.Face.area typed ticks² (S2-D).

fields.name: sectionModulus
fields.note: S = b·d²/6 (·ply) about this axis. Real in³.

fields.name: momentOfInertia
fields.note: I = b·d³/12 (·ply) about this axis. Real in⁴. Feeds BeamStatics deflection (E injected by strategy).

fields.name: axis
fields.note: strong | weak — which bending axis these properties describe (selected by member orientation downstream).

fields.name: ply
fields.note: Number of laminations (built-up members, e.g. 2-ply / 3-ply header). Multiplies A/S/I.

types.notes: Promoted to its single canonical home in materials (pure geometry derived from Dimensions). Real in²/in³/in⁴ single-sources the base-unit fix for solver (S2-D). No E / no design values (S1-A): geometry-only so it serves every material unchanged. crossSectionArea-vs-area split documented to prevent the very duplication the analysis flagged.

layers.purpose: The single home for every intrinsic, looked-up-by-key catalog in the system. Anything that is the same across thousands of placements — a species' design values, a purchasable SKU, a tool's stage list, a measurement grammar, a code provision, a prescriptive table, a book citation — lives here exactly once as a «reference» flyweight and is pointed at, never copied per instance. Contextual / per-placement data (the applied NDS factor stack, install orientation, grade stamp, per-piece source provenance) explicitly does NOT live here; it lives per-MemberPlacement / per-Piece in the placement/framing layers. This layer single-sources MechanicalProperties (resolving the 3x wood-design-value duplication S1-A) and makes the design-value flyweight material-extensible (forward-requirement 1) so cold-formed steel / concrete / masonry plug in WITHOUT core edits, and single-sources money (PriceQuote) so the bottom-up project estimate (forward-requirement 2) has one cost atom.
layers.baseUnitCompliance: Flyweight catalog data is intrinsic and largely non-geometric, so most fields are real-valued reference constants (psi/ksi/lb-ft3) — correct per the invariant, never ticks, and never area-as-ticks-squared (design strengths stay in native engineering units with an explicit unit field). No TickVec3/UnitVec3 appears in this layer because it carries no positions or directions — that is correct, not an omission; world geometry lives in the geometry-kernel layer. The ONLY linear fields here are spec/SKU lengths and they are int ticks @ 1/32in: SupplierSku.stockLength 3072 = 96in, StockSpec.nominalLengths each an int-ticks scalar. Money is real and currency-tagged (PriceQuote.unitPrice : float USD, PriceTier.pricePer : float USD) — never integer cents — resolving the solver's cents-vs-USD drift (S3-C). MeasurementGrammar.resolver produces world ticks for linear typed values, preserving the invariant at the point of entry.
layers.extensibilityNotes: New materials are added by adding leaf catalog rows, never by editing core types. The open seam is two-fold: (a) StockSpec carries a material + stockForm discriminator plus a generic specAttributes map, so a non-wood spec needs NO new fields on the shared type — the wood-only enums (species/grade/moistureCondition) are nullable denormalized convenience, and the canonical identity is the material-agnostic specAttributes map + mechanicalRef pointer; (b) MaterialDesignValueTable is the design-value seam: NDS_Wood / AISI_CFS / ACI_Concrete / TMS_Masonry are each a row keyed by material, each pointing (for wood) at MechanicalProperties or (for steel/concrete) at its own design-value VO, all interpreted behind the DesignStandard «interface» in the design-standard layer. Adding cold-formed steel = one AISI_CFS leaf row + its design-value VO + its specAttributes vocabulary; StockSpec.mechanicalRef (an opaque DesignValueKey) already accommodates it. stockForm (linear | sheet | cast) governs cut-vs-nest-vs-formwork so the cut/estimate optimizer extends to rebar and CFS studs without touching the spec type (concrete cast breaks the cut optimizer — gated by stockForm, not a special case). Estimating extends the same way: SupplierSku carries packaging + lead-time fields and references PriceQuote (a versioned money VO with bulk-break PriceTiers), so a new supplier, cost model (bulk tiers, regional pricing, freight), or unit-of-sale is new catalog data, not a schema change. PrescriptiveLookup/PrescriptiveTable reuse the reference-library subjectIndex shape verbatim, so a steel/concrete prescriptive table is the same lookup with a different CitationKey set.

S1-B (the contextual factor stack is excluded from this layer and relocated to per-MemberPlacement)
S2-C
S3-A (PriceTier, PrescriptiveTable, PrescriptiveRow, CitationKey all given real cards; ProvenanceLink deliberately NOT homed here — it is per-Piece contextual, belongs in framing/placement layer)
S3-C
S3-D (provenance kind vocabulary aligned: open|derived|verifyEdition)
S3-E (the «interface» Strategy seam is referenced, kept the only place material knowledge enters)

types.desc: SINGLE canonical source for a wood species+grade's intrinsic reference design values (NDS / AWC). Immutable value object, resolved FROM StockSpec by key, never copied per stud. Holds ONLY intrinsic reference strengths + stiffness + specific gravity. The applied adjustment-factor stack (CD/CM/Cr/CP/CL/Ci) is explicitly NOT here; it is contextual and lives per-MemberPlacement (AppliedFactors). CF (size factor), a property of the section, is supplied by the strategy at sizing time, not stored here.

types.desc: The material-extensible seam for design-value catalogs. A leaf flyweight, one row per (material, standard) family, keyed by DesignValueKey. For wood it points at MechanicalProperties; for steel/concrete it points at the material's own design-value VO. This is what makes the schema grow to new materials WITHOUT editing the core: add a leaf row, plug it behind the DesignStandard interface.

types.desc: Open registry key naming a material family (wood, cold-formed-steel, concrete, masonry, hot-rolled…). Replaces the closed `material` enum that was redeclared across material-blind core layers. A new material = a new registry row + a DesignStandard leaf; ZERO core edits. This is the keystone of requirement (1).

fields.name: key
fields.note: e.g. "wood" · "cold-formed-steel" · "concrete". Resolved through the registry, like StandardKey.

fields.name: standardKey

types.notes: Open key family (also: ProfileKey, QuantityBasisKey, ConnectionMethodKey, UomKey) — all material-specific vocabulary lives behind keys/leaves, never as closed enums in the core.

layers.purpose: The input layer (upstream of the geometry kernel) and the presentation adapter. Owns everything between the user's cursor and the DrawOp contract handed to the kernel: viewports/cameras, the single inference/snapping engine, the modal value-input grammar, the data-driven tool runner (the SketchUp tool set = one runner + a flyweight catalog, not N classes), construction guides, and snap provenance. It also hosts the JS-side render mirror (RenderMesh/MeshInstance/MaterialRef) extracted out of the materials domain schema. It never mutates canonical geometry: it emits DrawOp one-way to the kernel and reads back a read-only render mirror. All committed world points are TickVec3; view-math and screen-space remain float/px by design. The shared geometry primitives this layer used to own (Plane, CoordinateFrame) are relocated DOWN to the geometry-kernel; this layer keeps only the workspace-specific wrappers (SketchPlane) and references the rest.

layers.baseUnitCompliance: Committed world-space points are typed TickVec3 (geometry-kernel vec3<int> @ 1/32in ticks), upgrading the source schema's soft 'vec3 (resolved to tick)' prose to a hard type: SnapTarget.point, ReferenceAnchor.point, Tool.picks, ConstructionGeometry anchor, DrawOp payload, and SnapProvenance tick-offset are all tick-typed. Direction/normal fields are UnitVec3 (Camera.up, PickRay.dir, SketchPlane.axisU/axisV, ReferenceAnchor.impliedDir, ConstructionGeometry direction) — distinct from TickVec3 positions, never conflated. View-math fields (Camera.eye/target, PickRay.origin) and screen-space fields (Viewport.region, NavigationState.pan, PickRay.screenPt, SnapSettings.tolerancePx) remain float/px because they are legitimately not committed world geometry — the schema's own boundary line 'Pixel-defined, not world-defined; result resolves to world ticks' is preserved. NO area/volume/weight is stored in this layer at all, so there is zero opportunity for a ticks-squared violation here; RenderMesh.boundingBox references the geometry-kernel BoundingBox (real-derived extents) rather than restating it. ConstructionGeometry.geom no longer unions a position type with shape types — it is split into a tick-typed anchor + a UnitVec3 direction so an infinite-line/ray is (TickVec3 anchor, UnitVec3 dir) and a guide-point is just the anchor, never a malformed InfiniteLine|TickVec3|Ray union. This resolves the drawing soft base-unit violations from §3 of the audit.
layers.extensibilityNotes: Tools, grammars, and material appearances are all flyweight catalogs looked up by key (ToolDefinition, MeasurementGrammar, MaterialRef), so a new tool or a new material's render appearance is a new catalog row, never a core edit or a new class. Critically for the material-strategy seam: the render adapter is material-blind. RenderMesh keys geometry by geometryId and material by a MaterialKey, so when cold-formed-steel / concrete / masonry Stock arrives via the DesignStandard seam, it produces its own RenderMesh rows (a C-stud profile mesh, a generated formwork mesh) and MaterialRef rows (galvanized-steel, gray-concrete) with zero change to MeshInstance, the ToolRunner, or the inference engine — the presentation layer instances whatever the domain emits. DrawOp.kind is an open enum so new fabrication ops (steel-bend, concrete-pour) extend the contract additively. ToolDefinition.id and MeasurementGrammar.id are open key spaces for the same reason. SnapProvenance seeds associativity for future parametric-follow without schema change. For estimating: MeshInstance.domainId back-points to any Stock/Piece identity, so the takeoff/cost layer can pick a rendered instance and walk to its cost without the render layer knowing anything about price — render stays cost-blind, the seam is one uuid. Every newly-introduced helper type name (DrawOpPayload, the *Constraint set, the *Ref reference convention, the *Key catalog-key convention) is given an explicit canonicalHome so this layer introduces no new dangling references of its own.

types.desc: A screen region with its own camera and allowed interactions. Widescreen layouts hold up to 4; exactly one is active and drives the SketchPlane. The active viewport is the input focus — switching it re-targets where geometry lands.

fields.name: id
fields.note: Persistent identity; viewports survive layout saves. Promoted from bare to uuid per S3-B vocabulary.

fields.name: region
fields.note: Screen rectangle in layout space (x,y,w,h in float px). Screen-space, NOT world — legitimately float px. RectPx is a layer-local screen-space VO, NOT a geometry-kernel type (the kernel only knows ticks).

fields.name: active
fields.note: Only one Viewport true at a time; the input-focus flag.

types.notes: Unchanged from source group A. RectPx named explicitly so region is not a dangling inline shape.

types.desc: A screen-space rectangle in float pixels (layout space). Deliberately layer-local: pixels are a presentation concern, never world geometry, so this is NOT promoted to geometry-kernel. Distinct from any tick-based extent.
types.notes: NEW — closes the inline 'rect (px)' from the source. Screen-space px by design (audit: no fix). Kept out of geometry-kernel deliberately; the kernel is tick-only.

types.desc: Enumerates the fixed orthographic orientations plus free perspective. Locked types disable orbit; a locked view's U/V are its plane's two spanning axes (top→XY, front/back→XZ, left/right→YZ).

fields.name: lockedAxis
fields.note: null for perspective.

fields.name: allowsOrbit
fields.note: Perspective only.

types.notes: Unchanged from source.

types.desc: Eye/target/up plus projection. Orthographic for locked views (no perspective divide); perspective only where it earns its cost. One scene graph behind all of them — locked-view picking degenerates to an exact affine inverse, not a fuzzy raycast.

fields.name: id
fields.note: Promoted to uuid per S3-B.

fields.name: eye
fields.note: View-math position; legitimately float, NOT a committed world point (base-unit audit: explicit no-fix). Float because the camera lives between ticks, not on a tick lattice.

fields.name: target
fields.note: View-math; float by design.

fields.name: up
fields.note: Direction; geometry-kernel UnitVec3 (S3-B).

types.notes: Camera eye/target stay float per the audit — view math, not committed geometry. up retyped UnitVec3 (S3-B).

types.desc: Pan/zoom for every view; orbit for perspective only. Transient view state, persisted per viewport so a saved quad-split or perspective+elevations stack restores.

fields.name: zoom
fields.note: Scalar scale factor (unitless).

fields.name: pan
fields.note: Screen-space offset; float px by design (audit: no fix).

fields.name: orbit
fields.note: geometry-kernel quat (S3-B); null in locked views.

types.notes: orbit uses the shared quat primitive (S3-B); previously implicit. quat canonicalHome is geometry-kernel, referenced not redefined.

types.desc: Widescreen arrangement of 1–4 viewports. Saveable so a user's split persists across sessions.

fields.name: regions
fields.note: 1..4 viewports.

types.notes: Unchanged from source.

types.desc: The active drawing surface = the face under the cursor, or the ground datum for the first sketch. WRAPS the shared geometry-kernel Plane and adds local U/V so 2D picks lift to 3D exactly: lift(u,v) = plane.origin + u·axisU + v·axisV. Plane-locked ⇒ zero depth ambiguity; depth comes from the face.

fields.name: id
fields.note: SketchPlane has identity (the active one changes as you draw on faces); entity, so it carries a uuid.

fields.name: plane
fields.note: geometry-kernel Plane — NOT redefined here. The bare Plane primitive moved DOWN to geometry-kernel per S1-D; SketchPlane is the workspace-specific wrapper.

fields.name: axisU
fields.note: Local 2D x direction; UnitVec3, not a tick position.

fields.name: axisV
fields.note: Local 2D y direction.

fields.name: source
fields.note: FaceRef (face:uuid) into geometry-kernel Face, or the ground datum for the first sketch.

types.notes: Resolves S1-D: the bare Plane moves to geometry-kernel; SketchPlane stays here as the wrapper. Resolves the analysis note that materials/solver should not reach UP into drawing for the Plane primitive. NOTE on relocation: the source group B also defined CoordinateFrame here — per registry MISPLACED-PRIMITIVE it relocates to geometry-kernel (see layer-level note); it is NOT carded in this layer, and the inference engine references geometry-kernel:CoordinateFrame for its world basis. Stereotype is entity (the ACTIVE sketch plane has a mutable identity/lifecycle), distinct from the immutable value Plane it wraps.

types.desc: Screen point → world ray. Only the perspective view needs a true raycast; in a locked ortho view it collapses to a 2D inverse-transform (degenerate=true).

fields.name: origin
fields.note: Ray origin in view math — float, not a committed point (audit: no fix).

fields.name: dir
fields.note: Ray direction; UnitVec3 (S3-B).

fields.name: screenPt
fields.note: Originating screen pixel; float px by design.

fields.name: degenerate
fields.note: True in locked ortho — pick math is an exact affine inverse.

types.notes: Ray math is float by design (audit: no fix). Resolved world points it produces become TickVec3 downstream in SnapTarget — the px/tick boundary crossing happens at snap resolution, not here.

types.desc: The brain. Per frame: takes the pick + nearby geometry + active anchors/locks → best ranked SnapTarget. Written once in 3D, collapses to 2D for free under plane-lock. Recomputed every frame, never stored. Charged anchors feed back in as candidate sources (a charged midpoint emits from-point-along-axis guides, linear extension, perpendicular-from). Resolves axis-lock against geometry-kernel:CoordinateFrame in 3D, against the active SketchPlane U/V in a locked view.

fields.name: resolve
fields.note: The per-frame entry point. InferenceContext = the transient bundle {nearby geometry, active anchors, active lock, active SketchPlane} assembled per frame; it is a service input record, never persisted.

fields.name: candidates
fields.note: Ranked this frame; transient, recomputed every frame.

fields.name: worldBasis
fields.note: geometry-kernel:CoordinateFrame (global origin + X/Y/Z basis) — referenced, NOT redefined. This is where the source's CoordinateFrame (relocated out of group B per S2-B) is consumed: axis-lock resolves against it. canonicalHome geometry-kernel.

types.notes: Locality (architecture): the hot snap loop is the canonical split — JS feeds pick+screen state, Rust owns the candidate geometry; engine state is never persisted. FIX vs draft: removed the 'priority'/'consumes' pseudo-fields that duplicated SnapSettings.priority and re-listed the resolve() args — a service's contextual config (priority) belongs on the SnapSettings VO it reads, not copied onto the service. Added worldBasis so the relocated CoordinateFrame has a documented consumer here (S2-B) and is not silently dropped.

types.desc: One snap kind the engine can lock to — the full set the cursor recognizes. Arbitration when several fall inside tolerance: vertex > midpoint > center > intersection > onEdge > onFace, so snapping never feels random.

fields.name: kind
fields.note: [money = real decimal USD, single-sourced via PriceQuote — no cents]

fields.name: point
fields.note: BASE-UNIT FIX (S2-D drawing): committed/resolved world point is now hard-typed TickVec3, not the source's soft 'vec3 (resolved to tick)' prose.

fields.name: host
fields.note: What it snapped to; EdgeRef/FaceRef are geometry-kernel entity refs, GuideRef points at a ConstructionGeometry guide. *Ref = a typed uuid reference following the geometry-kernel ref convention (entityKind:uuid). null for pure-construction snaps like origin/onAxis.

types.notes: Resolves the drawing soft base-unit violation: point is TickVec3 (S2-D / S3-B). host union now explicitly admits null and names GuideRef's home (ConstructionGeometry).

types.desc: What the cursor locked onto this frame + why. Drives the on-screen glyph/color. Transient — never persisted; its persisted echo is SnapProvenance, written only at commit.

fields.name: color
fields.note: SnapColor = an rgba derived deterministically from target.kind (the source's 'by kind'); a layer-local presentation VO, not a dangling type.

fields.name: provenance
fields.note: Populated only on commit; null during hover.

types.notes: Transient render-facing value. FIX vs draft: replaced the undefined 'ColorByKind' with SnapColor and pinned it as a layer-local rgba VO so it is not a new dangling ref (S3-A discipline applied to this layer's own additions).

types.desc: A 'charged' point — hover a snap to arm it without placing a vertex. Carries the direction its host edge implies, enabling distance-from-anchor input: hover midpoint → arm anchor → edge supplies impliedDir → type d → place at anchor + impliedDir·d. Sign from cursor side.

fields.name: point
fields.note: BASE-UNIT FIX (S2-D drawing): the armed world point is TickVec3.

fields.name: impliedDir
fields.note: Edge direction (a UnitVec3 direction, NOT a position); null if the host has no direction.

types.notes: point retyped TickVec3 (S2-D). impliedDir is a UnitVec3 direction, not a position — the canonical TickVec3-vs-UnitVec3 distinction enforced.

types.desc: A snap or direction held so it survives cursor movement (hold key / arrow key). The single primitive behind axis-lock AND reference-distance AND 'lock to this edge' — all three are consumers of the same lock.

fields.name: constraint
fields.note: LockConstraint = the discriminated geometry to project onto, keyed by kind: a line/axis carries a (TickVec3 anchor, UnitVec3 dir); a plane carries a geometry-kernel:Plane ref; a point carries a TickVec3. All members reference kernel primitives — no geometry is re-declared here.

types.notes: FIX vs draft: the draft's 'LineConstraint|AxisConstraint|PlaneConstraint|PointConstraint' four-way union introduced four undefined types. Collapsed to one discriminated LockConstraint whose variants are described in terms of kernel primitives (anchor+dir / Plane / point) so nothing dangles and the base-unit typing of each variant is explicit.

types.desc: User-configurable tolerances and behavior. Tolerance is screen-space px, so zooming in buys precision for free. Pixel-defined, not world-defined — the result still resolves to world ticks.

fields.name: tolerancePx
fields.note: Per-kind px radius (point tighter than axis). Screen-space — legitimately px, NOT ticks (audit: explicit no-fix).

fields.name: priority
fields.note: Arbitration order + tie-break. This is the single canonical home for snap arbitration order; the InferenceEngine reads it, does not own a copy.

fields.name: angleSnap
fields.note: Increment in degrees, or null=off.

fields.name: grid
fields.note: Snap-to-tick grid toggle.

types.notes: Confirms the px/tick boundary the audit calls correct: tolerance px is the input, world ticks the output. Now the SOLE owner of snap priority (S6 single-source: the engine no longer duplicates it).

types.desc: The single modal text buffer (SketchUp's Measurements box). Opens on keypress mid-operation; the active tool stage parses it. Generic — the same buffer feeds distance, W×H, radius, angle, segment-count, array-count. Never build per-tool input widgets.

fields.name: buffer
fields.note: Live keystroke buffer.

fields.name: interpretedBy
fields.note: The current stage owns the grammar (via ToolStage.grammar).

fields.name: committed
fields.note: Set on Enter.

types.notes: Unchanged from source group D.

types.desc: Flyweight parse rules a stage points at. Defines tokens, separators, suffixes. Shared across tools that take the same kind of value; looked up by key, never copied per stage. 'Ns' (e.g. 10s) sets segment count; 'w,h' sets rectangle extents; bare number = primary scalar.

fields.name: id
fields.note: Opaque catalog key, e.g. 'lengthDir','wxh','radiusSeg'. GrammarKey = the layer's string-key convention for grammar catalog lookup — same flyweight pattern as LumberSpec/SupplierSku/ToolDefinition. Open key space.

fields.name: pattern
fields.note: TokenSpec = the declarative token grammar (e.g. d | w,h | r | Ns | angle); a value description of accepted tokens, intrinsic to the grammar row.

fields.name: resolver
fields.note: PURE intrinsic parse function (no per-instance state) — legitimate on a flyweight because it is shared behavior, not contextual data. Parses tokens → OpParams (the op-parameter record consumed by DrawOp construction); resolves dimensional input to TickVec3 deltas. ValueContext = active SketchPlane + anchors needed to interpret a relative value.

types.notes: Flyweight (S6 exemplar). Single-sourced here; the canonical home for measurement grammars. The resolver is intrinsic shared behavior (correct on a reference), distinct from any per-operation state which lives on Tool/ValueInput.

types.desc: The active editing mode = one generic runner holding a ToolDefinition + live runner state. Selecting a tool LOADS its ToolDefinition; it does not swap code. One class runs Line and Follow Me alike. State = where you are in the definition's stage list + what you've picked/locked. This is the single ToolRunner the architecture names.

fields.name: id
fields.note: The live runner instance has identity for the session; entity.

fields.name: definition
fields.note: Flyweight catalog entry (intrinsic behavior) — shared, never copied.

fields.name: stageIndex
fields.note: Current step into definition.stages (contextual runner state).

fields.name: anchors
fields.note: Charged anchors collected this operation (contextual).

fields.name: picks
fields.note: BASE-UNIT FIX: committed picks so far are TickVec3 (source had bare vec3[]).

fields.name: lock
fields.note: Active held lock, or null.

types.notes: Resolves the 'N tools = one runner + data catalog' mandate: Tool is the runtime entity (contextual runner state); ToolDefinition is the shared intrinsic flyweight. picks retyped TickVec3 (S2-D/S3-B). Intrinsic/contextual split is clean: nothing contextual (stageIndex, picks, anchors, lock) leaks onto the flyweight.

types.desc: One step of a tool: a prompt, what it picks, the constraint it imposes while moving, and the grammar its ValueInput accepts. Push/Pull = [pick face, drag-alongNormal + length]. Rectangle = [pick corner, pick opposite + 'w,h']. Follow Me = [pick profile face, pick path] → sweep. Same shape, different rows.

fields.name: prompt
fields.note: e.g. 'pick start', 'set bulge'.

fields.name: picks
fields.note: What this stage collects. 'path' generalizes to any future sweep tool.

fields.name: grammar
fields.note: Flyweight key into the MeasurementGrammar catalog (GrammarKey, matching MeasurementGrammar.id); null if the stage takes no typed value.

fields.name: emitsOnLast
fields.note: The DrawOp.kind emitted when this is the last stage; null otherwise. DrawOpKind = the open DrawOp.kind enum, referenced by name.

types.notes: FIX vs draft: grammar typed as GrammarKey (the actual key type) rather than the looser 'MeasurementGrammar.id'; emitsOnLast pinned to the named DrawOpKind enum. The data row that makes tools configuration not code.

types.desc: Flyweight catalog entry per tool. Pure data — the stage list + emitted op + cursor. Add a tool by adding a row, not a class. Same pattern as LumberSpec/SupplierSku: a shared catalog looked up by key, never instantiated per use.

fields.name: id
fields.note: Open catalog key, e.g. line|rect|rotRect|circle|polygon|arc2|arc3|pie|freehand|pushpull|move|rotate|followme|offset|scale. Open set — new materials/ops add rows. ToolKey = the tool catalog-key convention.

fields.name: produces
fields.note: The DrawOp.kind this tool emits (open enum).

fields.name: hotkey
fields.note: Optional keyboard shortcut; intrinsic to the tool definition (not per-instance), so correctly on the flyweight.

types.notes: Flyweight (S6 exemplar, cites LumberSpec lineage explicitly). Extensibility seam for the tool set — a new fabrication tool is a catalog row, no core edit. hotkey is intrinsic (no contextual leak).

types.desc: The committed result a tool emits — the ONE-WAY contract handed to the geometry kernel, lifted to 3D via the active SketchPlane. This is the boundary: workspace stops here; kernel consumes DrawOp → mutates Volume → re-solves framing. sweep ≠ move: Follow Me generates a solid along a path; Move only translates.

fields.name: kind
fields.note: OPEN enum — new fabrication ops (steel-bend, concrete-pour) extend additively for future materials. Referenced elsewhere as DrawOpKind.

fields.name: payload
fields.note: BASE-UNIT FIX (S2-D drawing): DrawOpPayload is a discriminated record whose geometry members are all tick-typed — points: TickVec3[], profile: geometry-kernel:Path2D (tick coords), delta: TickVec3, path: TickVec3[] — hard-typed instead of the source's prose 'in world ticks'. DrawOpPayload is a layer-local VO (the kind-discriminated op argument bundle); its geometry leaves reference geometry-kernel.

fields.name: plane
fields.note: The active plane used to lift 2D picks to 3D.

fields.name: targets
fields.note: geometry-kernel entity refs the op acts on (entityKind:uuid convention).

types.notes: Resolves DrawOp.payload soft base-unit (S2-D): DrawOpPayload is a named discriminated VO with tick-typed leaves, not prose. FIX vs draft: the draft left 'DrawOpPayload' undefined and typed the whole field 'ticks'; here it is named, its shape enumerated, and Path2D is sourced from the kernel rather than implied. The single point where the input layer hands off to the kernel; preserves one-way derivation.

types.desc: Tape-measure / protractor output: guide lines and points that exist ONLY to snap to. A distinct class so it NEVER promotes to building geometry — must not become a Volume or reach the framing solver, or stud counts inherit phantom geometry. The separate class makes the violation impossible.

fields.name: anchor
fields.note: BASE-UNIT FIX: the guide's world anchor point is a TickVec3 (source was untyped). For a guidePoint this IS the guide; for a guideLine/guideAxis it is the line's base point.

fields.name: dir
fields.note: Direction for guideLine/guideAxis (infinite line/ray = anchor + dir); null for guidePoint. A UnitVec3 direction, never a position.

fields.name: promotable
fields.note: Hard-false constant — boundary discipline; this object can never become a Volume or reach the solver.

types.notes: Boundary discipline preserved. FIX vs draft: the draft's geom:'InfiniteLine|TickVec3|Ray' illegally unioned a POSITION type (TickVec3) with two undefined shape types (InfiniteLine, Ray) and tagged the whole union 'ticks'. Split into a tick-typed anchor + a UnitVec3 dir so an infinite line/ray is correctly (anchor, dir) and a guide-point is just the anchor — no malformed union, no undefined InfiniteLine/Ray, correct TickVec3-vs-UnitVec3 separation. Deliberately non-promotable.

types.desc: Written on a committed vertex: what it snapped to, not just where. The seed of associativity (parametric follow: edge moves → dependent point follows). Distinct from materials.ProvenanceLink (supply-chain origin) and design-standard.Provenance (code-citation origin) — three deliberately different provenance concepts with deliberately aligned vocabulary.

fields.name: target
fields.note: Which snap kind produced the vertex.

fields.name: host
fields.note: The geometry-kernel entity snapped to (entityKind:uuid convention).

fields.name: param
fields.note: Parameter along edge (t in 0..1, unitless float) OR a tick offset (TickScalar = an integer tick distance). The two variants are explicitly different units; the offset variant is tick-typed (S3-B), never a bare number.

fields.name: anchorChain
fields.note: The charged anchors used to derive this vertex (enables parametric follow).

types.notes: Resolves S3-D: keeps the workspace 'snap origin' provenance distinct from the two other provenance concepts, with aligned naming. FIX vs draft: the draft's param 'float|int / t or ticks' let the tick variant be an untyped int — pinned to TickScalar so the tick offset is explicitly a tick-unit integer, not an ambiguous int. Cheap-now seed of associativity.

types.desc: INTRINSIC, shared, instanceable geometry — the flyweight half of the render mirror. A plain box scaled by length covers uncut stock; cut/angled pieces get generated geometry only when needed. Shared by many MeshInstance; deduped by geometryId. EXTRACTED out of the materials domain schema (layer 05) into this presentation adapter.

fields.name: geometryId
fields.note: Key for the shared GPU buffer — dedupes across instances. GeometryKey = the render-mesh catalog-key convention (the source's string geometryId). Flyweight lookup key.

fields.name: primitive
fields.note: box (uncut stock) | generated (cut/angled, or a CFS C-profile / concrete formwork mesh for future materials).

fields.name: lod
fields.note: low box ↔ high beveled (eased-edge bevel only at high — preserved from source).

fields.name: material
fields.note: Species/finish appearance key (MaterialKey) into the MaterialRef catalog — resolves S3-A dangling MaterialRef; the catalog is defined in this layer. Keyed (not embedded) so the appearance is shared, not copied per mesh.

fields.name: instanceable
fields.note: True for uncut stock; enables GPU instancing.

fields.name: boundingBox
fields.note: geometry-kernel BoundingBox (real-derived AABB extents) — NOT redefined here; resolves S2-B / MISPLACED-PRIMITIVE. null until computed.

types.notes: Resolves S2-A: render adapter moved OUT of materials (materials' own rule 'render lives outside the domain' now honored). Resolves S3-A: material is now a MaterialKey into the in-layer MaterialRef catalog. Flyweight split preserved — intrinsic geometry here, per-instance Transform in MeshInstance. boundingBox references kernel (S2-B). This IS the JS read-only render mirror; canonical geometry stays in the Rust SoA store. FIX vs draft: material typed as MaterialKey (the lookup key) not the MaterialRef object, so the flyweight is referenced by key not embedded by value — matching the geometryId pattern.

types.desc: One drawn occurrence: a reference to shared geometry + a per-instance Transform. The CONTEXTUAL half of the render flyweight — this is what gets batched into an instanced draw call. Maps from a Stock (formerly LumberStock) or Piece via domainId for picking and for the estimating layer's takeoff.

fields.name: meshRef
fields.note: Shared intrinsic geometry key (RenderMesh.geometryId / GeometryKey).

fields.name: transform
fields.note: Per-instance placement (instance matrix). geometry-kernel Transform — NOT redefined here; resolves S2-B. Transform.origin is TickVec3 ticks per the kernel.

fields.name: domainId
fields.note: Back-pointer to the domain Stock/Piece for picking AND for the estimating/takeoff walk (instance → cost) — render layer stays cost-blind. uuid per S3-B (source had 'UUID').

fields.name: lengthScale
fields.note: Non-uniform scale on the long axis for the box primitive; unitless scale factor (a contextual per-instance value, correctly here not on RenderMesh).

types.notes: Resolves S2-A (extracted from materials) + S2-B (Transform references kernel). Correct intrinsic/contextual split preserved: meshRef + material are intrinsic (shared), transform + lengthScale + domainId are contextual (per-instance). domainId is the seam to estimating without coupling render to price. References materials:Stock (the design-standard rename per S2-C), with LumberStock as documented alias.

types.desc: Flyweight catalog entry for a render APPEARANCE — species texture / color / finish, looked up by key (MaterialKey) and shared across every RenderMesh that uses it. Defines the type that was dangling in materials (S3-A). Material-blind to structural behavior: this is presentation only; the DesignStandard seam owns the engineering.

fields.name: id
fields.note: Opaque appearance key, e.g. 'spf-pine','galvanized-steel','gray-concrete','clay-brick'. New material → new row, no core edit. MaterialKey = the appearance catalog-key convention.

fields.name: baseColor
fields.note: Fallback flat color when no texture.

fields.name: texture
fields.note: Optional surface texture key into the R2-backed asset store; TextureKey = the asset-store key convention. null = flat baseColor only.

fields.name: finish
fields.note: Shading hint; null = engine default.

types.notes: Resolves S3-A dangling MaterialRef: defined HERE in the render adapter (the registry's prescribed fix), not in materials. Flyweight (S6) — the appearance catalog for any current or future material type, keeping the render mirror open to CFS/concrete/masonry without core edits. This is the material-strategy extensibility seam on the presentation side: it mirrors the DesignStandard seam (engineering) with a parallel appearance catalog (presentation), and the two never couple.

layers.purpose: Semantic promotion (kernel geometry -> Wall/Opening/Junction) followed by per-instance placement context. Turns shape into meaning, then captures HOW each member is installed. MemberPlacement is the canonical intrinsic/contextual seam: a shared StockSpec flyweight (renamed from LumberSpec, material-agnostic) plus per-instance install context (orientation, bracing, end conditions). This layer ORIGINATES the contextual adjustment-factor INPUTS as neutral physical facts (load-case linkage, member spacing, bracing, end fixity) but never models the factor stack or any material design values (that is the DesignStandard strategy seam) and never models the load traversal itself (that is the loads-analysis layer). Stud and member counts are always DERIVED by the FramingSolver, never stored. Extension assemblies (Floor, Roof, Sheathing) reuse the identical promotion + placement pattern so new assemblies land without restructuring; all are entities because they own identity and emit MemberPlacement[] through the shared solver.
layers.baseUnitCompliance: All linear geometry is integer ticks @ 1/32in (Wall.length/height/thickness, Opening.width/height/sillHeight/position, MemberPlacement.length, BracingRef.spacing, Floor.span, Sheathing.edgeSpacing). Positions/directions reference geometry-kernel named primitives: Wall.baseline is a kernel Segment (TickVec3 endpoints), MemberPlacement.transform is a kernel Transform (TickVec3 origin + quat), Orientation.strongAxis/weakAxis are UnitVec3 (unitless directions, NOT ticks). On-center module is stored as an integer tick count (16in=512t, 24in=768t) rather than a bare inch literal so the framer reads ticks uniformly; CRITICAL: fractional-inch OC modules must be stored as their exact tick value, not a fractional inch (19.2in = 614.4t is NOT an integer tick — it must be authored as the rounded/exact framer-agreed tick count, never as the float 19.2). No area/volume is stored anywhere in this layer: any derived wall/floor/sheathing face area is real in2 computed downstream, never ticks2. MemberPlacement.length and Wall.length both carry 'derived' notes so they are recomputed by the solver, not authored.
layers.extensibilityNotes: Floor/Roof/Sheathing are entity stubs that reuse the exact Wall promotion pattern (sourceFace -> assembly entity) and emit MemberPlacement[] through the same FramingSolver service keyed by assemblyKind, so no core type changes when they activate. MemberPlacement.specRef -> StockSpec is material-agnostic (StockSpec carries the material/stockForm discriminator in the materials layer), so cold-formed steel / hot-rolled studs, concrete formwork members, and masonry units all place through the identical contextual seam without editing this layer. MemberPlacement.role is an open string-coded enum (stud/joist/rafter/truss-chord/panel) rather than a closed wood-only set. The contextual factor INPUTS (load-case linkage, repetitive spacing, bracing, end fixity) are captured as neutral physical facts here; which factors (Cp/CL/CD/Cr or AISI/ACI analogs) those inputs drive is decided behind the DesignStandard interface seam downstream and which member-demand they pair with is resolved in the loads-analysis layer, so adding a material adds a strategy leaf and adding a load model adds a loads-analysis type, never a placement field. FramingSolver rule packs register per assemblyKind (truss, panelized) without new placement entities. The cost/estimating pipeline reads MemberPlacement[] (each carries specRef + derived length) as the bottom-up takeoff source without this layer knowing any cost model exists.
