types.desc: A bearing or partition wall semantically promoted from a vertical kernel Face. Carries the parameters the framer needs; stud count is DERIVED by FramingSolver, never stored. Edit the wall -> framer re-runs. The hard layer: meaning, not shape.

fields.name: sourceFace
fields.note: -> geometry-kernel Face this wall was promoted from. Re-promotion source when the face edits.

fields.name: baseline
fields.note: geometry-kernel Segment (2 TickVec3 endpoints) in plan. Replaces the solver's dangling inline Segment (S3-A).

fields.name: length
fields.note: DERIVED from baseline length; stored only as a fast read for OC layout, recomputed on edit. Not authored.

fields.name: thickness
fields.note: Nominal framed thickness (e.g. 2x4 wall = 3.5in = 112t).

fields.name: role
fields.note: Selects load participation and default detailing.

fields.name: spacing
fields.note: On-center module as an INTEGER tick count (16in=512t, 24in=768t). Stored as ticks not bare inches to keep the base-unit invariant uniform (S3-B); original solver field was '16|24 (in OC)'. Feeds Cr repetitive-member context downstream (S1-B). Fractional-inch modules (19.2in) must be stored as their exact agreed tick value, never as a float inch. [money = real decimal USD, single-sourced via PriceQuote — no cents]

fields.name: openings
fields.note: Voids owned by this wall; drive opening framing.

fields.name: junctionRefs
fields.note: Back-references to Junctions this wall participates in; ownerWall on the Junction resolves who frames the shared post.

types.notes: Stud count derived never stored (carried intact from solver). baseline/sourceFace now reference the extracted geometry-kernel primitives instead of solver-local definitions (S2-B, S3-A). spacing converted from inch literal to integer ticks (S3-B). junctionRefs added so wall->junction is navigable both ways (the original modeled only junction->wall).

types.desc: A door/window void in a wall. Drives king/jack/header/cripple/sill framing around it. Immutable VO owned by its Wall.

fields.name: width
fields.note: Rough-opening width, NOT door/window unit width. Header bearing length added by the framer per side.

fields.name: height
fields.note: Rough-opening height.

fields.name: sillHeight
fields.note: Height of rough sill above wall base; 0 for a door.

fields.name: position
fields.note: Offset of opening start measured along the wall baseline from wall start.

types.notes: Carried intact from solver; all four linear fields confirmed int ticks (already compliant).

types.desc: Where two or more walls meet. Names the condition AND the single owner so a shared post is counted exactly once. The #1 source of bad counts: both walls see the corner, only the owner frames it.

fields.name: walls
fields.note: All walls meeting at this junction.

fields.name: ownerWall
fields.note: The one wall that claims and frames the shared post. Resolves double-counting deterministically.

fields.name: method
fields.note: Corner/intersection framing method; selects how many members the owner emits.

types.notes: Owner-wall pattern resolves double-counting (sound DDD, carried intact). Kept as a value object per the source + registry (status ok): it is identified structurally by its participating walls, holds no independent lifecycle, and is recomputed when the walls move. Counts remain derived via the owner; nothing stored per non-owner wall.

types.desc: Wall + openings + junctions -> an ordered, STABLE set of MemberPlacements (plates, studs, opening framing, blocking) at the wall's OC spacing. A 2in nudge must not reshuffle every stud; anchor the grid, don't redraw it.

fields.name: in
fields.note: One wall (or one Floor/Roof/Sheathing assembly) plus the junctions it participates in (owner resolution applied).

fields.name: assemblyKind
fields.note: Selects the rule pack. Default wall; Floor/Roof/Sheathing register their own packs without new placement entities (extension seam). Open to new kinds (truss, panelized).

fields.name: rules
fields.note: OC layout, plate stack, opening framing, fireblock/blocking rules. Flyweight rule pack looked up by assemblyKind, not copied per wall.

fields.name: out
fields.note: Derived output. The single unit the structural check, the cut list, AND the cost takeoff all read.

fields.name: stability
fields.note: Policy that minimizes layout churn on small edits (grid anchoring).

types.notes: Produces MemberPlacement[] (derived). assemblyKind + RuleSetRef make this the single extension point for Floor/Roof/Sheathing (no restructuring). The in.wall slot generalizes to any assembly entity; crossRefs updated to name Floor/Roof/Sheathing as valid inputs so the extension seam is explicit, not implied.

types.desc: One framing member, in place. References a shared material-agnostic StockSpec; OWNS its install context. The exemplar intrinsic/contextual split. The unit the capacity check, the cut list, and the cost takeoff all read. Capacity = shared base values x factors derived from THIS placement's context.

fields.name: specRef
fields.note: -> StockSpec flyweight (materials layer; renamed from LumberSpec per S2-C). Material-agnostic: wood now, CFS/concrete/masonry later via the spec's material discriminator. Intrinsic, shared, never copied. Also the SKU/cost takeoff key read by the estimating pipeline.

fields.name: role
fields.note: Open enum: stud|king|jack|header|cripple|sill|plate|block (+ joist|rafter|chord|panel for Floor/Roof/Sheathing). String-coded so new assemblies extend roles without editing this type.

fields.name: transform
fields.note: geometry-kernel Transform (TickVec3 origin + quat rotation). Extracted primitive, not solver-local (S2-B).

fields.name: length
fields.note: Cut length, DERIVED by the solver from wall/opening geometry. Stored only as solver output, never authored; recomputed on edit.

fields.name: orientation
fields.note: Contextual: which face takes load -> strong vs weak axis.

fields.name: bracing
fields.note: Contextual: unbraced lengths per axis -> Cp/CL inputs (S1-B).

fields.name: ends
fields.note: Contextual: fixity at both ends -> effective-length factor + load transfer.

fields.name: connections
fields.note: -> ConnectionPoint[] (materials layer). Per-member fastener locations; feeds the design-standard ConnectionGraph aggregate AND the connection-based cost takeoff (fasteners/hardware).

fields.name: demandRef
fields.note: -> MemberDemand (loads-analysis layer): the per-member {axial,moment,shear} this placement carries, plus the governing load case. Neutral linkage; the placement does NOT compute or store loads. This is the contextual CD load-duration INPUT origin (S1-B) — replaces the draft's invented 'loadDurationContext' loose ref, single-sourcing loads to the loads-analysis layer.

types.notes: Exemplary intrinsic/contextual seam (carried + sharpened). Resolves S1-B: this layer captures factor INPUTS (demandRef->CD via load case, Wall.spacing->Cr, bracing->Cp/CL, ends->Ke) as neutral facts; it does NOT model the factor stack or design values (those are the strategy seam / materials flyweight) and does NOT model the load traversal (loads-analysis layer). FIX vs draft: the draft's 'loadDurationContext: load-duration-ref' invented a loose loads type inside the placement layer, duplicating the loads concern that the loads-analysis layer single-sources — replaced with demandRef -> loads-analysis.MemberDemand so loads stay in one home. Resolves S1-C: MemberPlacement feeds SizingArbiter, not the superseded flat CapacityCheck. Resolves S2-C: specRef -> StockSpec (renamed). Stud count never stored here; this is one already-placed member emitted by the solver. Cost note: specRef + length make each placement a takeoff line item without this type importing any cost model.

types.desc: The member's local axis frame. Decides strong vs weak axis - a ~5-6x swing in bending capacity for the same stick. Load on the wide edge = strong axis (joist); load flat on the narrow edge = weak axis.

fields.name: loadFace
fields.note: Which face takes load (narrow vs wide; e.g. 1.5in narrow vs 3.5in wide for a 2x4). Section-relative, material-agnostic — selects the section modulus that governs. Kept geometry-relative (not '1.5in vs 3.5in') so CFS/other sections place through the same field.

fields.name: strongAxis
fields.note: Unitless direction (geometry-kernel UnitVec3, NOT ticks). Was bare vec3 in solver (S3-B).

fields.name: weakAxis
fields.note: Unitless direction. Orthogonal to strongAxis; the axis the section is weak about.

types.notes: Resolves S3-B: strongAxis/weakAxis retyped from bare vec3 to UnitVec3 (directions, never tick positions). loadFace kept as narrow|wide (section-relative) so it stays material-agnostic per the extensibility requirement. Get this wrong and the section modulus inverts.

types.desc: What braces a given axis, and how often. Sets the effective (unbraced) length for column buckling (Cp) and beam stability (CL). The Cp/CL adjustment-factor INPUT originates here.

fields.name: axis
fields.note: Which member axis this bracing restrains.

fields.name: bracedBy
fields.note: Source of restraint. Sheathing typically braces the weak axis -> buckling governs about the other axis. none = full-height unbraced.

fields.name: spacing
fields.note: Unbraced length along the axis. Correct as ticks (linear). The physical input to Cp/CL; the factor is computed behind the seam (S1-B).

types.notes: spacing already correct as ticks (carried intact). Provides the Cp/CL contextual INPUT (S1-B) without modeling the factor itself. bracedBy=sheathing is the link the Sheathing entity supplies.

types.desc: Fixity at one end of the member. Feeds effective-length factor (Ke) and load-transfer mode. Bearing on a plate = Fc-perp crush check on the plate, axial on the stud; end condition picks the governing mode.

fields.name: end
fields.note: Which end of the member (aligned to baseline/transform direction).

fields.name: fixity
fields.note: Restraint condition. The effective-length-factor (Ke) and load-path INPUT; the factor itself is computed behind the seam (S1-B).

fields.name: connectionRef
fields.note: -> ConnectionPoint (materials layer) at this end. Ties end fixity to a concrete fastener/bearing detail and into the ConnectionGraph + connection cost takeoff.

types.notes: Carried intact. connectionRef points at the single materials.ConnectionPoint home (single-source); end fixity is a contextual factor input (Ke/Cp), consistent with S1-B.

types.desc: EXTENSION STUB. A floor/ceiling assembly promoted from a horizontal kernel Face. Reuses the Wall promotion pattern; joists/rims/blocking emit through the same FramingSolver (assemblyKind=floor). No core change when activated.

fields.name: sourceFace
fields.note: -> geometry-kernel Face (horizontal) this floor was promoted from.

fields.name: span
fields.note: Clear span; drives joist depth/spacing. Stub field.

fields.name: spacing
fields.note: Joist OC module as an INTEGER tick count (12in=384t, 16in=512t, 24in=768t). Feeds Cr context. 19.2in OC must be stored as its exact agreed tick value, never as the float 19.2in (=614.4t is not an integer tick). Stub field.

types.notes: Stub only - fields illustrative. Confirms Floor lands via the identical promotion + placement seam with zero restructuring (extension requirement). Added id so it is a proper entity. Fixed the 19.2in OC base-unit footgun in the spacing note (integer ticks only).

types.desc: EXTENSION STUB. A roof assembly promoted from sloped kernel Faces. Rafters/trusses/ridge emit through FramingSolver (assemblyKind=roof). Adds pitch; otherwise identical pattern to Wall/Floor.

fields.name: sourceFace
fields.note: -> geometry-kernel Face(s); sloped plane(s).

fields.name: pitch
fields.note: Rise/run (e.g. 6:12) — a unitless ratio, NOT ticks. Drives rafter length and bird's-mouth geometry. Stub field.

fields.name: spacing
fields.note: Rafter/truss OC module as an INTEGER tick count (16in=512t, 24in=768t). Feeds Cr context. Stub field.

types.notes: Stub only. Routes truss/rafter rule packs through FramingSolver.assemblyKind without new placement entities. pitch annotated as a unitless ratio so it is not mistaken for a tick value.

types.desc: EXTENSION STUB. A sheet-good skin (OSB/plywood/gypsum) applied to a Wall/Floor/Roof face. Promotes to MemberPlacements of role=panel AND supplies the dominant bracing source (bracedBy=sheathing) for the members it covers.

fields.name: appliesTo
fields.note: Assembly face this skin covers.

fields.name: panelSpecRef
fields.note: -> StockSpec for the sheet good (material-agnostic; carries panel material discriminator). Cost takeoff key for sheet goods. Stub field.

fields.name: edgeSpacing
fields.note: Fastener edge spacing in integer ticks; shear-capacity input. Stub field.

types.notes: Stub only. PROMOTED FROM value TO entity (fix vs draft): like Floor/Roof it owns identity, is referenced by BracingRef.bracedBy=sheathing, and emits MemberPlacement[] of role=panel through FramingSolver (assemblyKind=sheathing) — a thing that the solver consumes and that is referenced by other types is an entity, not an immutable VO. Added id and the FramingSolver crossRef to make the emit path explicit. Closes the loop between the skin and the Cp/CL bracing context without restructuring the placement layer; panelSpecRef keeps sheet goods on the same material-agnostic flyweight + cost-takeoff path.

layers.purpose: The NEW pipeline stage absent from every current strip: it sits between Placement and Structural Check and converts a placed framing model into per-member structural demand. It consolidates the four scattered load fragments (materials.Weight self-weight, solver.LoadCase buried inside the structural layer, design-standard.LoadContext + LoadCombination living in the design core) into one coherent concern. Load SOURCES (dead/live/snow/wind/seismic) are quantified per ASCE 7 / IRC; TributaryArea attributes area to each member; LoadPath walks the connection graph (sheathing -> stud -> plate -> header -> foundation) and LoadRollup accumulates demand down that path; LoadCombination (single-homed HERE, ASCE 7) factors the source set; and MemberDemand[] is the layer's only downstream product, consumed by the structural seam (SizingArbiter / the superseded CapacityCheck). The layer is material-blind: it produces axial/moment/shear/deflection demand AND the serviceability deflection LIMIT in real engineering units; WHICH combination governs is delegated to the DesignStandard strategy, so cold-formed steel / concrete / masonry plug in later without touching this core. LoadSolver orchestrates sources -> distribution -> combination -> demand as a dependency-graph recompute in the Rust/WASM worker. The layer is upstream of and cost-free for the estimating pipeline, but is the demand source that justifies member SIZES, so the bottom-up takeoff traces member quantity back through MemberDemand without this layer carrying any cost type.
layers.pipelineStage: loads & analysis (NEW STAGE, inserted between Placement and Structural Check)
layers.baseUnitCompliance: Linear geometry stays integer ticks @ 1/32in throughout: TributaryArea spans/widths, LoadPath segment lengths, member span/unbracedLength, and the deflection LIMIT denominator (computed from span in ticks) are all linear ticks; the load-application points are TickVec3. Everything DERIVED is real, never ticks-squared: tributary area is float in2 (this directly fixes S2-D, where solver.LoadCase.tributaryArea was typed ticks2); pressures psf; line loads plf; point loads lb; self-weight plf/lb (carried from materials.Weight, already real); spectral accel g; wind speed mph. The MemberDemand outputs (axial lb, moment lb-in, shear lb, deflection in, deflectionLimit in) are all real. TickVec3 (vec3<int>) is used ONLY for committed world positions (load application/centroid/node points in ticks); UnitVec3 (vec3 direction) ONLY for load/gravity/lateral direction vectors. They are never conflated and no area is ever typed ticks2 anywhere in this layer. Deflection is reported BOTH as an absolute value (in) and as its governing limit (in, derived from span/ratio) so the seam compares a real number against a real number, with no unit ambiguity.
layers.extensibilityNotes: The layer is deliberately material-blind so it stays open under the DesignStandard Strategy seam (forward requirement 1). Load SOURCES, TributaryArea, LoadPath, LoadRollup and MemberDemand know nothing about wood/steel/concrete - they speak psf/plf/lb/in. The material-sensitive decisions are pushed to the seam: (a) which LoadCombination governs is resolved by DesignStandard.governingCombination (wood picks min(load/CD), steel/concrete pick max factored) - LoadCombination carries the full ASCE 7 set but never decides; (b) CD load-duration is wood-only viscoelastic creep, so it is NOT computed here - LoadCombination exposes only the duration class (the cause), and the strategy maps class -> CD; (c) the deflection-limit RATIO (L/360, L/240) is a serviceability criterion surfaced here as the cause, while the deflection CAPACITY check (E-dependent) stays behind the seam in LimitStateCheck. Adding cold-formed-steel / concrete / masonry / hot-rolled requires zero edits to this layer: a new DesignStandard leaf consumes the same MemberDemand[] and picks its own governing combo and load-side-vs-resistance-side factoring (ASD vs LRFD is the cross-cutting DesignPhilosophy flag, single-homed in design-standard, not duplicated here). New ASCE 7 source types (rain R, flood Fa, ice) slot into the LoadSource discriminated union without changing rollup/combination/demand signatures. The estimating/cost forward requirement (2) is served WITHOUT a cost type here: takeoff joins on MemberDemand.memberRef -> MemberPlacement, never on a load field, so a future cost model attaches downstream with no edit to this layer. New load standards (a future ASCE 7 revision, local IRC amendments) attach via CodeProvision/CitationKey references, not new fields.
resolvesFindings: S2 missing-layer (a) Loads/Analysis: stands up the dedicated layer absent from every pipeline strip, inserted between Placement and Structural Check
S2-D base-unit: solver.LoadCase.tributaryArea typed ticks2 -> TributaryArea.area now real in2 (derived); all areas real, never ticks2; deflection limit derived from span-ticks but reported real in
collision misplaced-concern (S2): consolidates solver.LoadCase + designStandard.LoadContext into this layer; LoadCase/LoadContext removed from their old homes

collision duplicate-concept-misplaced (S2): LoadCombination single-homed HERE (pulled out of design-standard core), eliminating the duplicated ASCE 7 combo set; design-standard.LoadContext/LoadCombination and solver's implicit combos removed

S1-C competing-architecture (partial): produces a clean MemberDemand[] hand-off so the structural stage reconciles onto the DesignStandard seam (SizingArbiter) instead of solver's flat CapacityCheck reading raw LoadCase[]; MemberDemand carries memberRole + deflectionLimit so the seam selects the right LimitStateCheck set

S1-B intrinsic/contextual split: load-duration is contextual (per combination/use), surfaced as durationClass cause here and mapped to CD by the wood strategy - not folded into any intrinsic flyweight; live-load reduction inputs (KLL, At) retained so reductionFactor is recomputable, not a stale derived scalar

S3 price/weight provenance: reuses materials.Weight (real lb / plf) as the single self-weight source for DeadLoad rather than restating mass fields; resolves the orphaned-concern flag on materials.Weight by giving it a downstream consumer
S3-B primitive vocabulary: every world point typed TickVec3 (vec3<int>), every direction UnitVec3 (vec3) - no bare vec3 carried over from solver.LoadCase

types.desc: Permanent gravity load: self-weight of framing members referenced from materials.Weight, plus superimposed fixed assembly dead load (sheathing, finishes, MEP). Source 'D' in every ASCE 7 combination. Consolidates the only pre-existing load fragment (materials.Weight) into the loads layer as a first-class source. Self-weight is NOT restated as mass fields - it references materials.Weight (already real plf/lb).

fields.name: selfWeightPlf
fields.note: Member self-weight as a line load = materials-stock.Weight.weightPerFoot; the canonical bridge from the mass model into loads. NOT recomputed here.

fields.name: assemblyDeadPsf
fields.note: Superimposed dead of the assembly this member supports (sheathing + finish + ceiling + fixed MEP). ASCE 7 Table C3.1 / IRC typicals. Per-area, converted to force by TributaryArea in LoadRollup.

fields.name: direction
fields.note: Gravity direction (0,-1,0). UnitVec3 (vec3 dir) per base-unit invariant - a direction, never a tick position.

fields.name: sourceRef
fields.note: -> reference-library asce7 Table C3 / irc R301 dead-load provisions.

types.notes: No totalDead is stored: dead force = selfWeightPlf x span + assemblyDeadPsf x tributaryArea is derived inside LoadRollup (which alone owns span and tributaryArea), keeping DeadLoad a pure occupancy/material-blind value object and avoiding a derived field that references quantities this type does not hold.

types.desc: Transient occupancy load 'L'/'Lr', keyed by room/space use per IRC R301.5 (and ASCE 7 Table 4.3-1). Carries the inputs to the code-permitted live-load reduction for large tributary areas so reductionFactor is recomputable, not a stale scalar. Material-blind, occupancy-driven (reused across every member under that space).

fields.name: occupancy
fields.note: sleeping-room | living-area | attic-storage | deck | stairs | roof-Lr | ... -> selects basePsf per IRC R301.5.

fields.name: basePsf
fields.note: Unreduced uniform live load, e.g. 30 psf sleeping, 40 psf living, 20 psf Lr (IRC R301.5 / R301.6).

fields.name: isRoofLive
fields.note: Discriminates Lr (roof live) from L (floor live) - they enter different ASCE 7 combinations.

fields.name: elementFactorKll
fields.note: Live-load element factor KLL (ASCE 7 Table 4.7-1); an INPUT to the reduction, retained so reductionFactor can be recomputed rather than trusted blindly.

fields.name: reductionFactor
fields.note: Derived live-load reduction per ASCE 7 4.7 (from KLL and tributary At); 1.0 when not permitted. reducedPsf = basePsf x reductionFactor.

fields.name: reducedPsf
fields.note: Derived effective live load fed to combinations.

fields.name: sourceRef
fields.note: -> irc R301.5 / asce7 Table 4.3-1, 4.7.

types.notes: At (tributary area) is NOT duplicated here - the reduction reads TributaryArea.area, single-sourcing the geometry. Only KLL (an occupancy property) lives on LiveLoad.

types.desc: Roof snow load 'S' derived from ground snow per ASCE 7 Ch. 7 (pf = 0.7 Ce Ct Is pg, plus slope factor Cs and drift). Material-blind source; a value object reused across roof members.

fields.name: groundSnowPg
fields.note: pg from ASCE 7 Fig 7.2-1 / local jurisdiction map - the site input.

fields.name: exposureCe
fields.note: Exposure factor (ASCE 7 Table 7.3-1).

fields.name: thermalCt
fields.note: Thermal factor (Table 7.3-2).

fields.name: importanceIs
fields.note: Snow importance factor (Table 1.5-2).

fields.name: slopeCs
fields.note: Roof-slope reduction factor (7.4).

fields.name: flatRoofPf
fields.note: Derived flat-roof snow: 0.7 x Ce x Ct x Is x pg.

fields.name: designSnowPsf
fields.note: Derived sloped/drifted design snow fed to combinations.

fields.name: sourceRef
fields.note: -> asce7 Ch. 7.

types.desc: Wind pressure 'W' per ASCE 7 (Directional Ch. 27 or Envelope Ch. 28 for low-rise; WFCM prescriptive alternative). Produces directional pressures applied to surfaces; both an MWFRS lateral demand and a C&C uplift component. Material-blind. Carries both lateral (MWFRS) and uplift (C&C) so LoadCombination can build both gravity and net-uplift cases.

fields.name: basicSpeedV
fields.note: Ultimate design wind speed V (ASCE 7 Fig 26.5-1) - site input.

fields.name: exposureCategory
fields.note: B | C | D (ASCE 7 26.7).

fields.name: velocityPressureQz
fields.note: Derived qz = 0.00256 Kz Kzt Kd Ke V^2 (26.10).

fields.name: mwfrsPressurePsf
fields.note: Main wind-force-resisting-system pressure (lateral) -> LoadPath lateral demand.

fields.name: ccUpliftPsf
fields.note: Components & cladding uplift -> reverses gravity sign in uplift combos (0.6D+0.6W).

fields.name: direction
fields.note: Wind direction unit vector; lateral, not gravity. UnitVec3 (vec3 dir) per invariant.

fields.name: method
fields.note: directional | envelope | wfcm-prescriptive - the wfcm path lets IRC houses skip full ASCE 7 calc.

fields.name: sourceRef
fields.note: -> asce7 Ch.26-28 / wfcm.

types.desc: Seismic base shear 'E' per ASCE 7 Ch. 11-12 ELF (V = Cs W). Lateral source; for light-frame residential often governed by wind, but modeled for completeness and high-SDS sites. Material-blind (R is a lateral-SYSTEM property supplied alongside, not a material constant resolved here).

fields.name: sds
fields.note: Design spectral accel, short period (ASCE 7 11.4) - site input.

fields.name: sd1
fields.note: Design spectral accel, 1-sec period.

fields.name: responseR
fields.note: Response modification coefficient for the lateral system (Table 12.2-1), e.g. 6.5 for light-frame wood shear walls. A system property, not a material constant.

fields.name: importanceIe
fields.note: Seismic importance factor (Table 1.5-2).

fields.name: seismicWeight
fields.note: Effective seismic weight W = dead + applicable partition/snow per 12.7.2 (sources rolled in).

fields.name: baseShearV
fields.note: Derived V = Cs x W, Cs = SDS/(R/Ie).

fields.name: direction
fields.note: Lateral seismic direction. UnitVec3 (vec3 dir).

types.desc: The plan/elevation area a single member carries, derived from spacing and span geometry. Converts a psf source pressure into a per-member plf line load or lb point load. THE fix for S2-D: area is real in2 (derived), NEVER ticks2 as solver.LoadCase had it; the linear spans/widths that derive it stay integer ticks. Pure geometry->area conversion; psf x area = force happens in LoadRollup, keeping this a clean value object.

fields.name: memberRef
fields.note: -> building-placement.MemberPlacement; the member this area is attributed to.

fields.name: span
fields.note: Member span (clear/effective length) in int ticks @1/32in - linear, stays ticks.

fields.name: tributaryWidth
fields.note: Half-spacing to each adjacent member (e.g. 16in OC -> 16in trib width). Int ticks - linear. [money = real decimal USD, single-sourced via PriceQuote — no cents]

fields.name: area
fields.note: DERIVED real area = (span/32) x (tributaryWidth/32). EXPLICITLY real in2, never ticks2 (fixes loads-analysis.LoadCombination.tributaryArea : ticks2, S2-D). Also the At input to LiveLoad.reductionFactor - single-sourced here, not restated on LiveLoad.

fields.name: shape
fields.note: strip (joist/stud) | rectangular (girder) | triangular (one-way to ridge) | polygon - selects the area integration rule.

fields.name: loadCentroid
fields.note: Application point of the resultant in world ticks (vec3<int>). TickVec3, distinct from any direction vector. [money = real decimal USD, single-sourced via PriceQuote — no cents]

types.desc: Walks the connection graph downward (sheathing -> stud -> plate -> header -> foundation), determining for each node which downstream member(s) receive its load and in what share. This is the traversal that was missing from every schema - nothing owned the path. Reads the first-class connection graph; emits an ordered, acyclic sequence LoadRollup folds over. A verb (graph walk), not retained state: orderedNodes is its computed output, not stored model state.

fields.name: graph
fields.note: -> design-standard-seam.ConnectionGraph (nodes=Piece[]/MemberPlacement[], edges=Connection[]). The single graph LoadPath traverses; no parallel graph built here - single-sources connectivity.

fields.name: root
fields.note: Starting node (typically a sheathing/diaphragm or top-of-stack member); omitted -> path computed for all gravity roots.

fields.name: terminal
fields.note: foundation | bearing-wall | beam | shearwall-chord - where the walk stops (load delivered to support).

fields.name: orderedNodes
fields.note: DERIVED topological order sheathing->stud->plate->header->foundation; the sequence LoadRollup folds over. Output of the walk, not stored state.

fields.name: shareRule
fields.note: tributary | stiffness | rigid-diaphragm | flexible-diaphragm - how a node splits load among multiple downstream edges.

fields.name: loadDirection
fields.note: gravity vs lateral path - gravity follows bearing stack, lateral follows the diaphragm-to-shearwall path. UnitVec3 (vec3 dir).

fields.name: isLateral
fields.note: True for the wind/seismic lateral path (diaphragm -> shearwall -> hold-down -> foundation), distinct from the gravity bearing path.

types.notes: Reuses designStandard.ConnectionGraph rather than redefining connectivity, and consumes materials.ConnectionPoint via its edges. Acyclicity is a precondition (a cycle = an unstable/over-constrained model surfaced as a check failure).

types.desc: Folds source loads down a LoadPath, accumulating demand at each node: each member carries its own tributary load plus everything delivered from upstream. Owns the span x plf and area x psf force arithmetic (the only place span and tributaryArea meet a pressure). Produces, per member and per source, the UNFACTORED accumulated reaction that LoadCombination then factors. Separating rollup (geometry/statics) from combination (code factors) keeps the layer material-blind and the combos single-sourced.

fields.name: path
fields.note: The ordered traversal to fold over.

fields.name: sources
fields.note: The Dead/Live/Snow/Wind/Seismic value objects in scope (LoadSource = the tagged union of the source VOs).

fields.name: tributaries
fields.note: Per-member trib areas converting psf -> per-member force during the fold.

fields.name: accumulated
fields.note: DERIVED per-member, per-source accumulated load {memberRef, sourceKind, axial lb, lineLoad plf, moment lb-in, shear lb, reaction lb}. Unfactored - combination happens next. Reaction at each node feeds the next node down the path.

fields.name: method
fields.note: simple-bearing (statically determinate tributary) | continuous-beam (multi-span reactions) - selects the reaction model at each node.

types.notes: Outputs UNFACTORED accumulated demand keyed by source so LoadCombination can apply ASCE 7 factors. AccumulatedDemand is the rollup's internal record (real lb/plf/lb-in); MemberDemand is the FACTORED, governing-combo product downstream.

types.desc: Discriminated-union tag over the source value objects (DeadLoad | LiveLoad | SnowLoad | WindLoad | SeismicLoad). Lets LoadRollup, LoadCombination and LoadSolver pass a heterogeneous source set without the structural seam knowing the concrete subtype. Keeps the layer open to new ASCE 7 source types (rain R, flood Fa, ice) without changing rollup/combination/demand signatures - the extensibility seam for forward requirement 1.

fields.name: kind
fields.note: dead | live | roof-live | snow | wind | seismic | (extensible: rain | flood | ice). The ASCE 7 load symbol D/L/Lr/S/W/E.

fields.name: payload
fields.note: The concrete source VO. Adding a new source = new enum value + new VO; no signature changes upstream/downstream.

fields.name: effect
fields.note: gravity | lateral | uplift - replaces loads-analysis.LoadCombination.direction (axial|lateral|gravity), preserving the gravity-vs-lateral-vs-uplift distinction that routes the source to the correct LoadPath. Whether a gravity load becomes axial (stud) or bending (joist) is resolved per-member by MemberDemand.memberRole, not flattened here.

types.notes: Extensibility seam for forward requirement 1: new material standards never edit the sources, and new ASCE 7 sources slot in here without touching rollup/combination/demand.

types.desc: An ASCE 7 load combination - SINGLE-HOMED HERE (resolves the duplicate-concept-misplaced collision: pulled out of design-standard core, removed from solver). Carries the full combination SET (1.4D; 1.2D+1.6L+0.5Lr; 1.2D+1.0W+L+0.5Lr; 0.9D+1.0W; 0.6D+0.6W for ASD) but does NOT decide which governs - delegated to DesignStandard.governingCombination per the seam.

fields.name: comboId
fields.note: Stable combo identifier, e.g. 'LRFD-2' or 'ASD-7'. Named comboId (not 'id') to avoid colliding with the MODEL-shape type id.

fields.name: philosophyRef
fields.note: -> design-standard-seam.DesignPhilosophy (ASD | LRFD). Selects ASCE 7 2.3 (LRFD strength) vs 2.4 (ASD allowable) tables. REFERENCED, not redefined: DesignPhilosophy is single-homed in design-standard (it decides load-side vs resistance-side factoring); this layer only points at it.

fields.name: terms
fields.note: Ordered factored {sourceKind, factor} terms, e.g. [{dead,1.2},{live,1.6},{roof-live,0.5}]. The factored recipe.

fields.name: durationClass
fields.note: permanent | ten-year | two-month | seven-day | ten-minute | impact - the CAUSE of CD. CD itself is wood-only and computed by the NDS strategy, NOT here (S1-B: contextual factor surfaced as cause, not folded into any intrinsic flyweight).

fields.name: includesUplift
fields.note: True for net-uplift combos (0.9D+1.0W / 0.6D+0.6W) where wind reverses the dead-load sign.

fields.name: sourceRef
fields.note: -> asce7 Section 2.3 (LRFD) / 2.4 (ASD).

types.notes: CANONICAL home for ASCE 7 combinations; design-standard.LoadContext/LoadCombination and solver's implicit combos are removed. The set is shared/material-blind; governing selection is delegated to the strategy (wood: min(load/CD); steel/concrete: max factored). philosophy is referenced (not duplicated) to keep the ASD/LRFD flag single-sourced - this is a value object that POINTS at the cross-cutting flag, it does not own it.

types.desc: One factored term inside a LoadCombination: a load-source kind and its ASCE 7 factor. Promotes the inline {sourceKind, factor} pair to a named VO (analogous to the registry's PriceTier promotion, danglingRefs fix) so the combination recipe is inspectable and the factor table stays single-sourced.

fields.name: sourceKind
fields.note: dead | live | roof-live | snow | wind | seismic - matches LoadSource.kind.

fields.name: factor
fields.note: ASCE 7 load factor, e.g. 1.2 (D, LRFD), 1.6 (L), 0.6 (D in uplift/ASD).

fields.name: companionFactor
fields.note: Reduced factor when this source is a companion (e.g. 0.5L when wind/seismic leads), per ASCE 7 combination rules.

types.desc: The layer's ONLY downstream product: per-member factored structural demand under the governing combination - {axial, moment, shear, deflection} plus the serviceability deflection LIMIT and the member ROLE that selects which limit states apply. Consumed by the structural seam (DesignStandard.SizingArbiter, superseding solver.CapacityCheck's raw LoadCase[] input). Material-blind real engineering units; the seam compares this against material capacity. Carries demand only (no capacity, no pass/fail) - capacity and utilization live behind the seam in LimitStateCheck.

fields.name: memberRef
fields.note: -> building-placement.MemberPlacement; the member this demand sizes. Also the JOIN KEY by which downstream estimating/takeoff traces member quantity - no cost field needed here.

fields.name: memberRole
fields.note: column | beam | bearing | tension-tie | brace - selects WHICH LimitStateCheck set the seam applies (column->buckling, beam->bending+deflection). Replaces the lost solver.CapacityCheck.mode coupling; makes axial-vs-bending explicit instead of inferred from which fields are nonzero (closes the gap left by collapsing LoadCase.direction into a bool).

fields.name: governingCombo
fields.note: Which ASCE 7 combo produced this demand (chosen by DesignStandard.governingCombination - recorded, not decided here).

fields.name: axial
fields.note: Factored axial demand (compression +, tension -). For studs-as-columns / tension ties.

fields.name: moment
fields.note: Factored max bending moment, e.g. wL2/8 from BeamStatics. For headers/joists-as-beams. lb-in (real), not ticks.

fields.name: shear
fields.note: Factored max shear, e.g. wL/2.

fields.name: deflection
fields.note: Service-load deflection magnitude (real in) from BeamStatics 5wL4/384EI; reported alongside its limit so the seam need not re-derive geometry. E is injected by the strategy, so this is the geometry/load part the seam completes.

fields.name: deflectionLimit
fields.note: DERIVED serviceability limit = span_in / ratio, real in. Recovers the L/360 (live) and L/240 (total) criteria from BeamStatics that the draft dropped: deflectionRatio (the cause) lives here; the E-dependent CAPACITY check stays behind the seam. Computed from span (ticks/32) but reported real in - no ticks ambiguity.

fields.name: deflectionRatio
fields.note: The serviceability denominator (e.g. 360 for live, 240 for total) the deflection limit applies; the contextual cause, mapped per occupancy/use, not a material constant.

fields.name: uniformLoad
fields.note: The governing factored line load w that BeamStatics integrates - passed through so the seam can recompute moment/shear/deflection with its injected E and section.

fields.name: span
fields.note: Effective span for statics, int ticks (linear). The seam divides by 32 for in. THE linear quantity; deflectionLimit/deflection are its real derivations.

fields.name: unbracedLength
fields.note: Lateral-unbraced length for CL/CP (the seam applies the buckling factor; surfaced here as geometry). Int ticks (linear).

fields.name: reaction
fields.note: Support reaction delivered to the next member down the LoadPath (closes the rollup at the terminal). Recorded from LoadRollup.accumulated.reaction for the governing combo.

fields.name: appliedAt
fields.note: Resultant application point in world ticks (vec3<int>). TickVec3, distinct from any direction.

types.notes: This clean hand-off is what lets the structural stage reconcile onto the DesignStandard seam (S1-C) instead of solver's flat check reading raw LoadCase[]. memberRole + deflectionRatio/deflectionLimit are the contextual cues the seam needs to pick limit states and serviceability criteria WITHOUT this layer knowing any material capacity - preserving material-blindness.

types.desc: Orchestrates the layer end-to-end: quantify sources (1) -> TributaryArea + LoadPath + LoadRollup distribution (2) -> apply LoadCombination set (3) -> emit MemberDemand[] (4). Runs as a dependency-graph recompute in the Rust/WASM worker over the canonical SoA tick arrays; only re-derives demand for members whose placement/geometry/source inputs changed (incremental, like the rest of the engine). The single entry point the architecture's 'Loads & analysis' Rust machine maps to.

fields.name: in
fields.note: Placed members (from FramingSolver), the connection graph to traverse, and the active site/occupancy-driven sources.

fields.name: standard
fields.note: -> the active strategy; LoadSolver asks it governingCombination (and durationClass->CD mapping) so the layer stays material-blind. The plug-in seam for forward requirement 1.

fields.name: out
fields.note: Per-member factored demand -> the structural check stage (SizingArbiter).

fields.name: recompute
fields.note: incremental (dirty-range driven) | full - incremental is the default; only members touched by a changed input re-derive.

fields.name: dirtyMembers
fields.note: Inputs that changed since last run -> scopes the recompute. Mirrors the engine-wide dirty-propagation model (system-architecture.DirtyRange).

types.notes: Delegates the material-sensitive decisions (governing combo, CD, deflection capacity) to DesignStandard so adding CFS/concrete/masonry/hot-rolled needs no edits here (forward requirement 1). Emits to the structural stage only; carries no cost type, so the estimating pipeline joins downstream on MemberDemand.memberRef (forward requirement 2) without touching this service.

layers.purpose: The future-proofing core of the unified schema (forward requirement #1). One material-blind structural-check stage that calls THROUGH a single DesignStandard interface; N material leaves supply behind it. The shared core (BeamStatics M=wL²/8, SizingArbiter, LimitStateCheck records, PrescriptiveLookup) never moves when material changes; each leaf (NDS_Wood, AISI_CFS, ACI_Concrete, AISC_Steel, TMS_Masonry) supplies exactly six things plus its prescriptive/connection tables: design values, modification factors, a LimitStateCheck[] set, a connection-capacity method, the governing load combination, and the section basis. Adding a material is adding a leaf, never editing the core. This layer SUBSUMES solver.{NDSDesignValues, AdjustmentFactors, CapacityCheck} (resolves S1-A/S1-B/S1-C): the flat NDS-specific solver model is re-expressed as the NDS_Wood leaf behind the seam plus the material-blind SizingArbiter/BeamStatics core. Loads (solver.LoadCase / design-standard.LoadContext / LoadCombination) are NOT modeled here — they are consumed from the dedicated loads-analysis layer (resolves the misplaced-concern direction of the S2 loads collision); only the per-strategy PICK of the governing combination lives here.
layers.baseUnitCompliance: Linear geometry never originates here — it arrives via geometry-kernel primitives (geometry-kernel.Dimensions carries the int-tick member dimensions; this layer reads them only to derive REAL section quantities). No TickVec3/UnitVec3 appears in this layer at all — it carries no positions or directions, only scalar engineering quantities, so the vec3<int>-vs-vec3 distinction is honored vacuously (nothing to mistype). SectionProperties.A/S/I are real in²/in³/in⁴ — the explicit counter-example to solver.Face.area's ticks² (resolves S2-D); area is NEVER typed ticks². Strength/stress design values are stored canonically in real psi (the CANONICAL injected unit into BeamStatics); leaf lookup tables that quote ksi (e.g. steel E=29,000 ksi, Fy 33/50 ksi) are converted ×1000 to psi at the leaf boundary, exactly as span converts from ticks to inches — the canonical injected unit is single and unambiguous (psi), the table unit is a leaf detail. Moments real lb·in; shears real lb; deflection limits expressed as ratios (L/360, L/240) resolved to a real-inch limit. Utilization ratios are unitless reals ≤ 1.0. Span L consumed by BeamStatics arrives as a real-inches value derived from the member's int-tick length (1in = 32 ticks) at the call boundary, not stored here. Money/cost does not live in this layer (it is produced downstream from supplier SKUs); this layer emits only structural pass/fail + sized section. meta restates the tick invariant to resolve S3-F (design-standard previously declared no base unit).
layers.extensibilityNotes: New material = new leaf implementing the DesignStandard interface; zero edits to Tier-1 core. The boundary test is mechanical: a field belongs behind the seam iff it reads Fb-or-Fy / ASD-or-LRFD / gross-or-effective / cut-or-cast. The interface declares a finite, enumerable supply surface (six methods + section basis + philosophy + prescriptive/connection tables), so a leaf author has a closed checklist (the field-map). AISI_CFS, ACI_Concrete, AISC_Steel, TMS_Masonry are stubbed but structurally complete — every interface method present, fields typed, only the lookup tables unpopulated. DesignPhilosophy (ASD|LRFD) is a cross-cutting value carried by SizingArbiter, not a per-material constant, so a hybrid project (wood walls + steel beams + concrete footing) runs each member through its own leaf under one arbiter — the real end state the seam exists for. stockForm (geometry-layer Stock entity, referenced here) is the FOUR-VALUE routing enum linear | sheet | cast | unit: linear→cut list + offcut pool, sheet→nest, cast→formwork+volume, unit→unit count + grout/mortar volume — so concrete and masonry bypass the linear cut optimizer without the core knowing about either material (gotcha #3); the core branches on stockForm, never on material. LimitStateCheck.origin = core|strategy lets a leaf ADD failure modes (web crippling, distortional buckling, rebar dev length, masonry slenderness) without the arbiter enumerating them. PrescriptiveLookup is the material-blind non-engineered path: a leaf supplies its own prescriptive table (IRC stud height, AISI prescriptive, ACI minimums) behind the same lookup shape, so adding a material adds a table, not arbiter code — and SizingArbiter.escape=engineeredDesign fires only when no prescriptive entry covers the query. For the cost/estimating requirement (#2): this layer's SizingResult.sizedSpec + ConnectionGraph (fastener counts) + stockForm (routing) feed downstream takeoff — the seam produces the sized section and the joint topology, the estimating layer prices them from supplier SKUs; no cost types leak into the structural core, and no structural types leak into the cost layer.

types.desc: THE seam. The single Strategy interface every Tier-1 core type calls through to stay material-blind. Each material leaf implements it, supplying exactly six things: design values, modification factors, a LimitStateCheck[] set, a connection-capacity method, the governing load combination, and the section basis — plus its prescriptive table. The 6th DDD stereotype («interface»), formally adopted in the shared legend (resolves S3-E), reusing the render color slot but distinct. Resolves S1-C (subsumes solver's flat structural model) and closes the design-standard dangling I/O refs DesignValues/Factor/SizingQuery/Result (S3-A) by single-sourcing them as the strategy I/O types defined in this layer.

fields.name: id
fields.note: Opaque key-string alias, e.g. 'NDS-2018', 'AISI-S100-16'. Selects the leaf (documented key alias per S3-A, not a defined type).

fields.name: code
fields.note: NDS | AISI | AISC | ACI | TMS — which standard this leaf implements. Must enumerate all five to match the user's forward material list.

fields.name: material
fields.note: wood | steel | concrete | masonry — discriminator; must match the Stock.material it sizes. (steel covers both AISI cold-formed and AISC hot-rolled.)

fields.name: sectionBasis
fields.note: gross | effective | transformed | cracked | net | plastic — tells SectionProperties which basis to compute. Wood=gross, CFS=effective, concrete=transformed/cracked, hot-rolled=gross/plastic, masonry=net/transformed.

fields.name: philosophy
fields.note: The leaf's DEFAULT philosophy (wood→ASD, steel/concrete→LRFD). SizingArbiter may carry a per-project override; gotcha #2.

fields.name: designValues
fields.note: Supply ①: resolve a spec to a SectionBasis bundle (strengths + stiffness on the declared basis). For wood this returns a SectionBasis whose designValuesRef points at the single reference-flyweights.MechanicalProperties flyweight; the leaf does NOT restate Fb/Fv/E — resolves S1-A.

fields.name: modificationFactors
fields.note: Supply ②: the applied (contextual) factor stack for a given placement/use condition. Single home for the factor stack — resolves S1-B; subsumes solver.AdjustmentFactors and the reference-flyweights.MechanicalProperties.adjustmentFactors map (which is REMOVED from the flyweight).

fields.name: limitStates
fields.note: Supply ③: the set of material failure modes this leaf checks, each tagged origin=strategy. Core injects bending/shear/deflection (origin=core); leaf adds CP, local/distortional/LTB, web crippling, rebar dev length, masonry slenderness, etc.

fields.name: connectionCapacity
fields.note: Supply ④: capacity of a typed ConnectionGraph edge (Connection). Wood=nail/screw yield Z (EYM); CFS=screw shear/pullout, weld; concrete=rebar development, lap splice; masonry=anchor bolt. Resolves the dangling 'connectionCapacity' I/O AND the dangling ConnectionGraph edge type 'Connection' (S3-A).

fields.name: governingCombination
fields.note: Supply ⑤: pick which ASCE 7 combination governs. Wood=min(load/CD); steel/concrete=max factored. The combination SET is shared and owned by the loads-analysis layer; only the PICK is per-strategy (gotcha #1). Loads are NOT modeled here.

fields.name: prescriptiveTable
fields.note: Supply ⑥ (optional): the leaf's non-engineered prescriptive table (IRC stud height, AISI/ACI prescriptive minimums). Material-blind lookup shape, reused from reference-library subjectIndex. Carries over design-standard's PrescriptiveLookup that the draft dropped; pairs with SizingArbiter.escape.

fields.name: implementations
fields.note: Catalog of known leaves: NDS | AISI | AISC | ACI | TMS. Documentation/registry only.

types.notes: Boundary rule (the test for what belongs behind the seam): if a field reads Fb-or-Fy / ASD-or-LRFD / gross-or-effective / cut-or-cast, it lives HERE, not in Tier-1 core. The interface has a finite, enumerable supply surface so adding a material is a checklist, not a redesign. Strategy I/O types (SectionBasis←DesignValues, ModificationFactor←Factor, LimitStateCheck, ConnectionCapacity, SizingQuery, SizingResult←Result, PrescriptiveLookup←PrescriptiveTable, Connection) are all single-sourced in this layer — closing every design-standard dangling ref from S3-A.
crossRefs: materials.MechanicalProperties (wood designValues flyweight, referenced not restated — S1-A)
designStandard.StockSpec (input to designValues; the renamed LumberSpec, S2-C)

types.desc: Cross-cutting ASD|LRFD flag — NOT a per-material constant. Decides whether safety factors hit the LOAD side (ASD combinations) or the RESISTANCE side (LRFD φ). Carried by SizingArbiter; each leaf declares its default. Resolves gotcha #2 and the design-standard DesignPhilosophy card (carriedBy=SizingArbiter).

fields.name: mode
fields.note: ASD | LRFD

fields.name: factorSide
fields.note: load (ASD — factors live in the load combinations) | resistance (LRFD — φ reduces capacity).

types.notes: Immutable VO. NOTE: per-limit-state safety/resistance factors (Ω=1.67 bending vs different for shear; φ=0.90 flexure vs 0.75 shear) are NOT scalar fields here — a single Ω/φ would be wrong, since they vary by failure mode. They are emitted per-mode as ModificationFactor entries (appliesTo=load|resistance) and applied in LimitStateCheck.capacity. This VO carries only the MODE, which is the genuinely cross-cutting flag. A hybrid project runs wood (ASD) and steel (LRFD) members under one SizingArbiter — the arbiter reads each member's leaf default unless explicitly overridden. This is exactly why philosophy is a flag, not a hardcoded core assumption.
crossRefs: designStandard.ModificationFactor (per-mode Ω/φ live here, not as scalars on DesignPhilosophy)

types.desc: Material-blind orchestrator of the structural-check stage. Holds NO design values. Takes a SizingQuery + the selected DesignStandard, runs BeamStatics for demand, asks the strategy for capacity (design values × modification factors, per the strategy's LimitStateCheck set and governing combination), and returns a SizingResult. Identical code for wood, steel, concrete, masonry — only the injected strategy differs. SUPERSEDES solver.CapacityCheck (resolves S1-C).

fields.name: size
fields.note: The core entry point. Material-blind: calls THROUGH the interface for every material-specific value. Replaces solver.CapacityCheck — resolves S1-C.

fields.name: philosophy
fields.note: Cross-cutting ASD/LRFD flag the arbiter carries (gotcha #2). Defaults to the strategy's declared philosophy; may be overridden per project.

fields.name: statics
fields.note: The shared statics engine; arbiter feeds it span + load + (strategy-injected) E/I.

fields.name: prescriptiveFirst
fields.note: When true, the arbiter tries the strategy's PrescriptiveLookup before closed-form sizing (IRC/AISI/ACI prescriptive path). On a miss it falls back to BeamStatics + limit states, or raises escape.

fields.name: escape
fields.note: ok | engineeredDesign — raised when the query falls outside BOTH the prescriptive table AND the closed-form envelope and needs an engineer. Carries over solver's prescriptive-vs-engineered split; gated by PrescriptiveLookup coverage.

types.notes: The arbiter is the keystone of material-blindness: it never branches on material (it branches only on stockForm for routing and on origin for check aggregation). It enumerates LimitStateChecks the strategy supplies, compares each demand to capacity, and aggregates the governing utilization. Adding a material adds checks via the leaf's limitStates() and rows via its PrescriptiveLookup, not via arbiter edits.

types.desc: Pure statics, material-blind. M=wL²/8, V=wL/2, Δ=5wL⁴/384EI. E and I are INJECTED by the strategy/section; this service owns no material knowledge. Public-domain mechanics identical across all materials. No analog existed in solver (solver lacked an explicit statics service) — this is the material-blind demand half of what solver.CapacityCheck conflated.

fields.name: moment
fields.note: M = wL²/8 for simple span. w in lb/in (plf÷12), L in inches (member int-tick length ÷ 32). Real, never ticks.

fields.name: shear
fields.note: V = wL/2.

fields.name: deflection
fields.type: (w: real, L: real, E: real, I: real) -> real
fields.note: Δ = 5wL⁴/384EI. E (psi) and I (in⁴) injected from the strategy's SectionBasis + SectionProperties. Result a real-inch deflection.

fields.name: deflectionLimit
fields.note: Resolves L/360 (live) or L/240 (total) to a real-inch allowable. ratio chosen by use/limit state.

types.notes: Boundary-test clean: every input/output is real engineering units, zero material conditionals, zero ticks. The span L is the only linear input and arrives already converted to real inches at the call site (1in=32 ticks), honoring the base-unit invariant without storing ticks here. E is always injected in psi (the canonical unit); leaves quoting ksi convert ×1000 at the leaf boundary.

types.desc: Uniform demand-vs-capacity record. The single shape every failure-mode check produces, whether the core supplies it (bending/shear/deflection) or the strategy does (CP, web crippling, rebar dev length, masonry slenderness). Replaces solver.CapacityCheck.mode enum with a first-class, extensible, per-mode record. origin tag is what lets a leaf ADD modes without the core enumerating them. Resolves S1-C (the cleaner re-modeling of solver's flat 'mode').

fields.name: id
fields.note: bending | shear | deflection | columnBuckling | bearing | localBuckling | distortionalBuckling | LTB | webCrippling | crackedFlexure | stirrupShear | devLength | splice | axialSlenderness | flexuralTension | compactness — OPEN enum; leaves extend it (origin=strategy).

fields.name: demand
fields.note: Applied effect for this mode (real engineering units: lb·in for bending, lb for shear/axial, in for deflection). From BeamStatics or the strategy.

fields.name: capacity
fields.note: Allowable/factored resistance for this mode. designValue × Π(ModificationFactor.value where appliesTo=resistance for LRFD φ) for ASD: designValue × factor stack ÷ Ω; capacity already incorporates the per-mode Ω/φ as ModificationFactor entries, NOT a global DesignPhilosophy scalar.

fields.name: ratio
fields.note: demand/capacity, unitless. ≤ 1.0 passes. Governing utilization is max(ratio) across the set.

fields.name: origin
fields.note: core (shared: bending/shear/deflection, capacity injected by strategy) | strategy (material-specific mode supplied wholly by the leaf).

fields.name: pass
fields.note: Derived: ratio ≤ 1.0. Convenience for the arbiter's aggregation.

types.notes: Shared (origin=core): bending, shear, deflection — capacity injected. Strategy (origin=strategy): CP column buckling + Fc⊥ bearing (wood); local/distortional/LTB + web crippling (CFS); cracked-section flexure + stirrup shear + dev/splice length (concrete); LTB/flange-local/compactness (hot-rolled); axial slenderness + flexural tension (masonry). The uniform shape is what makes the arbiter material-blind. Per-mode Ω/φ enters via the ModificationFactor stack feeding capacity.

types.desc: The strategy's resolved design-value + stiffness bundle for a spec, on the correct basis (gross/effective/transformed/cracked/net/plastic). The strategy's designValues() output type — single-sources the dangling 'DesignValues' ref (S3-A). For wood this WRAPS A REFERENCE to materials.MechanicalProperties; it does not restate Fb/Fv/E (resolves S1-A — single-sourced wood design-value flyweight).

fields.name: basis
fields.note: gross | effective | transformed | cracked | net | plastic — must equal the leaf's DesignStandard.sectionBasis. THE single owner of the basis discriminator (SectionProperties merely records the resolved value).

fields.name: designValuesRef
fields.note: For wood: opaque key-string alias into the reference-flyweights.MechanicalProperties flyweight (the single canonical wood design-value source). Strengths NOT copied here — resolves S1-A. Null for materials whose values are code constants (e.g. steel E=29,000 ksi).

fields.name: strengths
fields.note: Resolved characteristic strengths on the basis, keyed by id (Fb/Ft/Fc/FcPerp/Fv for wood; Fy/Fu for steel; f′c/fy for concrete; f′m/fy for masonry). Canonical unit psi; leaf ksi tables convert ×1000 at the boundary. For wood these are READ from the MechanicalProperties flyweight via designValuesRef, not stored independently.

fields.name: E
fields.note: Modulus of elasticity injected into BeamStatics. Wood→from MechanicalProperties.E; steel→29,000,000 psi const; concrete→Ec. Canonical psi (ksi tables ×1000 at boundary).

fields.name: Emin
fields.note: Stability/buckling modulus (NDS Emin). Wood-relevant; null where the material uses a different buckling formulation (e.g. CFS uses effective-width, not Emin).

fields.name: section
fields.note: A, S, I on the declared basis. For effective/cracked the strategy reduces gross before populating.

types.notes: This is the crux of single-sourcing (S1-A): the wood leaf points at materials.MechanicalProperties via designValuesRef rather than re-declaring Fb/Ft/Fc/FcPerp/Fv/E/Emin a third time. The basis discriminator is OWNED here (not duplicated independently on SectionProperties) — the strategy decides gross vs effective vs cracked, the core SectionProperties just computes and records the geometry on that basis.
crossRefs: materials.MechanicalProperties (wood, by key — S1-A single source)

types.desc: ↳ Defined in **materials-stock**. Referenced from this stage. Pure section geometry: area A, section modulus S, moment of inertia I (plus plastic Zx whe

One applied (contextual) modification factor in the strategy's stack. The single canonical home for the factor stack — resolves S1-B and the dangling 'Factor' ref (S3-A). Subsumes solver.AdjustmentFactors, the materials.MechanicalProperties.adjustmentFactors map, and design-standard.modificationFactors. Intrinsic/contextual split (S1-B): the FACTOR VALUE applied to a member is mostly contextual (depends on load case, spacing, bracing), so factors live per-placement, NOT folded into the intrinsic MechanicalProperties flyweight.

fields.name: id
fields.note: Wood: CD | CM | Ct | CF | CFu | Ci | Cr | CP | CL. CFS: phi | omega | Q(effectiveWidth) | holeReduction. Concrete: phiFlex | phiShear | creep | rhoMin | rhoMax. HotRolled: phi | omega | Cb. Masonry: phi | slendernessReduction. OPEN enum; leaves extend.

fields.name: value
fields.note: Multiplier applied to the base design value (e.g. CD=1.0, CF=1.1, φ=0.90, Ω=1.67). Unitless.

fields.name: kind
fields.note: intrinsic | contextual. Only CF (size) is genuinely intrinsic to the section; CD/Cr/CP/CL/φ/Q are contextual (load duration / spacing / bracing / use). Resolves S1-B's split: contextual factors must NOT be stored in the flyweight.

fields.name: appliesTo
fields.note: load | resistance — which side the factor hits, consistent with DesignPhilosophy.factorSide. THIS is where per-mode Ω (load-side, ASD) and φ (resistance-side, LRFD) live — not as scalars on DesignPhilosophy.

fields.name: limitState
fields.note: If this factor is mode-specific (φ=0.90 flexure vs 0.75 shear; Ω=1.67 bending), the LimitStateCheck.id it applies to. Null for stack-wide factors (CD applies to all). Lets the arbiter route the right φ/Ω to the right check.

fields.name: source
fields.note: What contextual input selected this factor (the load case for CD, spacing for Cr, unbraced length for CP/CL). Null for intrinsic factors.

types.notes: Capacity = base design value × Π(factor.value) on the applicable side/mode. Solver got the contextual nature right ('role selects the stack, derived from placement'); materials wrongly folded the whole stack into the intrinsic flyweight. This type single-sources it AND tags intrinsic-vs-contextual so the storage location is unambiguous (S1-B). Per-mode Ω/φ are ModificationFactor rows (appliesTo + limitState), which is why DesignPhilosophy carries only the mode flag. CD is wood-only (viscoelastic creep) — the type is shared but the leaf decides which ids it emits.
crossRefs: solver.MemberPlacement (contextual source — where the applied stack now lives, per S1-B)
materials.MechanicalProperties (intrinsic base values; the adjustmentFactors map is REMOVED from it, per S1-B)

types.desc: The contextual inputs a strategy needs to select the applied factor stack — the request type for modificationFactors(). Makes the intrinsic/contextual split (S1-B) explicit: everything here is per-member install context, not intrinsic spec data.

fields.name: loadDuration
fields.note: permanent | tenYears | twoMonths | sevenDays | tenMinutes | impact — selects wood CD. Comes from the governing load case, not the stick.

fields.name: moistureCondition
fields.note: dry | wet — selects CM. Service condition, contextual.

fields.name: repetitive
fields.note: True for members ≥3 @ ≤24in OC (studs 16in OC) — selects Cr. Depends on spacing/use, not the member alone.

fields.name: unbracedLength
fields.note: Le for column (CP) / beam (CL) stability — from bracing context (building-placement.BracingRef). Derived REAL inches from int-tick bracing spacing (÷32) at the boundary.

fields.name: flatUse
fields.note: Loaded on the flat — selects CFu. Orientation context.

fields.name: incised
fields.note: Pressure-treatment incising — selects Ci.

types.notes: Sourced from solver.MemberPlacement (orientation, bracing, ends) and the loads-analysis governing case — exactly the per-instance contextual data S1-B says must stay out of the flyweight. The strategy reads this to produce ModificationFactor[]. unbracedLength is the only linear field and is carried as REAL inches (converted from int ticks), never ticks here.

types.desc: First-class connection graph: nodes = Piece[] (members), edges = Connection[] (typed joints). The aggregate that materials.ConnectionPoint per-member fastener locations feed into. Carries over the design-standard ConnectionGraph entity that the draft omitted — it is REQUIRED for the cost/estimating requirement (#2): fastener counts per edge are a bottom-up takeoff input. Identity persists across recompute (it is the building's joint topology), so «entity», not «value».

fields.name: id
fields.note: Opaque key alias; identity persists across solver recompute.

fields.name: nodes
fields.note: The connected members. References materials-stock.Piece (carry-over, by key) — not restated here.

fields.name: edges
fields.note: Typed joints between nodes. Each is a Connection (defined in this layer) — closes the dangling 'Connection' edge-type ref (S3-A).

types.notes: Connection EXISTENCE/topology is shared and material-blind (lives here); connection CAPACITY is per-strategy (ConnectionCapacity via DesignStandard.connectionCapacity). Same intrinsic/contextual discipline as the rest of the seam. The edge count × fastener-per-edge feeds estimating takeoff (#2) without any cost type entering this layer.

types.desc: One typed joint edge in the ConnectionGraph: a fastened connection between two members, typed by joint + method, with per-instance fastener count. Closes the design-standard dangling 'Connection' ref (S3-A). The material-blind topology half; ConnectionCapacity is the per-strategy capacity half.

fields.name: method
fields.note: nailYield | screwShear | screwPullout | boltYield | boltBearing | weld | rebarDevelopment | lapSplice | anchorBolt | dowel — the joint type, supplied/validated by the leaf's connectionCapacity().

fields.name: count
fields.note: Fasteners in this joint. Unitless count — a primary bottom-up takeoff quantity for estimating (#2).

fields.name: fastenerLocations
fields.note: References materials-stock.ConnectionPoint (int-tick positions) — not restated here; the geometry stays in materials.

types.notes: Topology + count are material-blind and live here; the leaf supplies the capacity computation. Per-instance contextual data (count, locations) stays on the edge, not on any shared flyweight — flyweight discipline preserved.

types.desc: Output of the strategy's connectionCapacity() — the allowable/factored capacity of one typed Connection. Material-specific computation (wood EYM yield, CFS screw, rebar development) behind a uniform shape. Resolves the dangling 'connectionCapacity' I/O (S3-A) and pairs with Connection/ConnectionGraph.

fields.name: method
fields.note: Mirrors Connection.method (nailYield | screwShear | screwPullout | boltYield | weld | rebarDevelopment | lapSplice | anchorBolt | dowel) — the joint type, material-determined.

fields.name: Z
fields.note: Nominal/allowable capacity per fastener (wood EYM Z, CFS screw shear, etc.). Real force.

fields.name: groupFactor
fields.note: Cg / row-reduction for multiple fasteners. Unitless. Total joint capacity = Z × Connection.count × groupFactor.

types.notes: The strategy supplies the method and the numbers; ConnectionGraph supplies the topology and count. This keeps connection EXISTENCE shared and connection CAPACITY per-strategy — the same intrinsic/contextual discipline as the rest of the seam. (count moved to Connection, where the per-instance quantity belongs, instead of being duplicated on this capacity VO.)
crossRefs: designStandard.Connection (the edge this is the capacity of)

types.desc: ↳ Defined in **reference-flyweights**. Referenced from this stage. Material-blind prescriptive (non-engineered) table lookup — IRC stud height, AISI/ACI pres

The request to SizingArbiter.size() — a member's demand + candidate section + selected standard. Closes the dangling 'SizingQuery' ref (S3-A). Consumes loads-analysis.MemberDemand (the per-member {axial, moment, shear} the loads layer produces), keeping loads out of this layer (resolves the misplaced-concern direction for solver.LoadCase / design-standard.LoadContext).

fields.name: memberId
fields.note: Which placed member is being sized (building-placement.MemberPlacement). Opaque key alias.

fields.name: demand
fields.note: Per-member {axial, moment, shear} from loads-analysis.MemberDemand (referenced by key, not restated). Loads are NOT modeled here — consumed from the dedicated loads layer.

fields.name: candidateSpec
fields.note: The trial section/spec (materials-stock.StockSpec, the renamed LumberSpec per S2-C). Opaque key alias. Arbiter checks if it passes; may iterate to the next size.

fields.name: span
fields.note: Clear span in REAL inches, converted from the member's int-tick length (÷32) at the call boundary. Feeds BeamStatics. Never stored as ticks here.

fields.name: context
fields.note: Contextual inputs for the factor stack (S1-B).

types.notes: References StockSpec (the renamed LumberSpec, S2-C) by KEY and MemberDemand (loads-analysis) by key, so this layer cleanly depends on the placement/loads layers without absorbing their concerns or restating their types. span carried as real inches honors the base-unit boundary.

types.desc: The arbiter's output — pass/fail with governing utilization, the full LimitStateCheck set, and the sized section. Closes the dangling 'Result' ref (S3-A). The handoff to downstream estimating (forward requirement #2): the chosen StockSpec + section + connection topology feed bottom-up takeoff without cost types leaking into the structural core.

fields.name: pass
fields.note: True iff every LimitStateCheck.ratio ≤ 1.0 (or a prescriptive table entry covers the query).

fields.name: governingCheck
fields.note: The LimitStateCheck.id with max utilization — the binding constraint.

fields.name: utilization
fields.note: max(ratio) across checks, unitless. The number engineers care about.

fields.name: checks
fields.note: Full demand/capacity record per mode (core + strategy), for transparency and report export.

fields.name: sizedSpec
fields.note: The spec that passed (= candidate, or the arbiter's next size up). Opaque key alias. Feeds cut optimization + supplier + estimating downstream — the bridge to requirement #2.

fields.name: method
fields.note: closedForm | prescriptive — which path produced the result (BeamStatics+limit states vs PrescriptiveLookup). Provenance for the check.

fields.name: philosophyUsed
fields.note: Which ASD/LRFD basis produced this result — provenance for the check.

fields.name: escape
fields.note: ok | engineeredDesign — set when out of BOTH prescriptive and closed-form envelope (carried from SizingArbiter).

types.notes: sizedSpec is the deliberate seam to the estimating layer: the structural check decides WHAT section, the takeoff layer prices it from supplier SKUs. No PriceQuote/cost field appears here, keeping the structural core cost-blind and the cost layer structurally-blind. Cost extensibility (#2): a new cost model is added downstream by consuming sizedSpec + ConnectionGraph; this type never changes.

types.desc: The wood leaf — the reference implementation, FULLY POPULATED. Implements DesignStandard for material=wood, code=NDS, philosophy=ASD. SUBSUMES solver.{NDSDesignValues, AdjustmentFactors} (resolves S1-C) and single-sources its design values by REFERENCING materials.MechanicalProperties rather than restating them (resolves S1-A — the third copy is eliminated). A shared flyweight: one NDS catalog looked up by spec key, never copied per member.

fields.name: id
fields.note: 'NDS-2018' (or edition). Opaque key alias.

fields.name: designValuesRef
fields.note: Key into reference-flyweights.MechanicalProperties (Fb, Ft, Fc, FcPerp, Fv, E, Emin, G). NOT restated here — resolves S1-A. Seed: DF-L No.2 → Fb 900, Fv 180, E 1.6e6, Emin 580k, G 0.50 (psi).

fields.name: specKeys
fields.note: species | grade | sizeClass — the lookup dimensions into the NDS Supplement design-value tables.

fields.name: factorIds
fields.note: CD, CM, Ct, CF, CFu, Ci, Cr, CP, CL — the factor ids this leaf emits as ModificationFactor[]. CD wood-only (viscoelastic creep). Subsumes solver.AdjustmentFactors.

fields.name: limitStatesExtra
fields.note: columnBuckling (CP), bearing (Fc⊥ crush) — the origin=strategy checks wood adds beyond core bending/shear/deflection.

fields.name: sectionBasis
fields.note: gross — wood uses the gross section.

fields.name: connectionMethod
fields.note: nailYield | screwYield — EYM (European Yield Model) Z values.

fields.name: prescriptiveTable
fields.note: IRC/WFCM wood span + stud-height tables — the non-engineered path. Reference to a PrescriptiveLookup.

fields.name: philosophy
fields.note: ASD; governingCombination picks min(load/CD).

fields.name: stockForm
fields.note: linear (dimensional lumber, → cut list + offcut pool) + sheet (WSP/plywood, → nest). One of the four routing values linear|sheet|cast|unit.

types.notes: Already built — this is what the sizing-engine layer implements. The key move: designValuesRef points at the ONE canonical MechanicalProperties flyweight in materials, so correcting a species table corrects it everywhere (resolves S1-A drift). solver.NDSDesignValues + AdjustmentFactors collapse into this leaf behind the seam (S1-C).

types.desc: Cold-formed steel leaf — the cheapest next material, same wall topology as wood. Stubbed but structurally complete: every DesignStandard method present and typed, only the AISI S100 tables unpopulated. Adding it requires zero core edits — the proof of requirement #1.

fields.name: id
fields.note: 'AISI-S100-16'. Opaque key alias.

fields.name: designValues
fields.note: Fy (33/50 ksi yield), Fu, E = 29,000 ksi — code constants (no MechanicalProperties flyweight needed; designValuesRef null). ksi converts ×1000 to psi at the boundary (canonical injected unit psi).

fields.name: specKeys
fields.note: mil/thickness | yield(33|50) | profile (track/stud/header).

fields.name: factorIds
fields.note: phi | omega | Q(effectiveWidth) | holeReduction. φ/Ω per LRFD/ASD (emitted as ModificationFactor with appliesTo+limitState); Q is the CFS effective-width reduction.

fields.name: limitStatesExtra
fields.note: localBuckling, distortionalBuckling, LTB, webCrippling — the CFS-specific failure modes.

fields.name: sectionBasis
fields.note: effective — S_eff < gross (the defining CFS departure from wood; SectionProperties computes the reduced section).

fields.name: connectionMethod
fields.note: screwShear | screwPullout | weld.

fields.name: prescriptiveTable
fields.note: AISI prescriptive (AISI S230) stud/header tables — stubbed.

fields.name: philosophy
fields.note: LRFD (or ASD) · max factored governs.

fields.name: stockForm
fields.note: linear → cut list + offcut pool (track/stud/header port from wood with terminology swaps).

types.notes: Track/stud/header, king/jack, braced walls all port from wood with terminology swaps. The hybrid case (wood walls + steel beams + concrete footing) is the real end state — the argument for the seam. Stub = tables empty, structure complete.

types.desc: Concrete leaf — included to prove the seam HOLDS where assumptions break (cast, not cut; transformed/cracked section). Stubbed but structurally complete. Demonstrates the stockForm routing: concrete bypasses the linear cut optimizer entirely without the core knowing about concrete (gotcha #3).

fields.name: id
fields.note: 'ACI-318-19'. Opaque key alias.

fields.name: designValues
fields.note: f′c (concrete strength, psi), Ec (modulus, psi), rebar fy. designValuesRef null (code/mix constants).

fields.name: specKeys
fields.note: barSize | cover | mix.

fields.name: factorIds
fields.note: phiFlex(0.90) | phiShear(0.75) | creep | rhoMin | rhoMax — strength-reduction + reinforcement ratio limits (emitted as ModificationFactor, phi with limitState).

fields.name: limitStatesExtra
fields.note: crackedFlexure, stirrupShear, devLength, splice — concrete-specific modes.

fields.name: sectionBasis
fields.note: transformed | cracked — the section geometry itself changes under load (the deepest departure from wood/steel gross/effective).

fields.name: connectionMethod
fields.note: rebarDevelopment | lapSplice.

fields.name: prescriptiveTable
fields.note: ACI minimum-reinforcement / cover prescriptive — stubbed.

fields.name: philosophy
fields.note: LRFD strength design · max factored governs.

fields.name: stockForm
fields.note: cast → formwork + volume, NOT a cut list. Routes to a formwork/volume output, bypassing the linear cut optimizer + offcut pool (gotcha #3). One of the four routing values linear|sheet|cast|unit.

types.notes: The hardest leaf — proves the seam survives a material whose section is non-gross, whose output is volumetric, and whose optimizer path is entirely different. The core never branches on 'concrete'; stockForm=cast does the routing. Stub = tables empty, structure complete.

types.desc: Hot-rolled structural steel leaf (wide-flange beams, HSS columns). Stubbed but structurally complete. The natural partner to CFS in the hybrid end state (steel beams spanning wood/CFS walls). Listed in the interface's implementations enum; included so the leaf set matches the user's forward material list.

fields.name: id
fields.note: 'AISC-360-16'. Opaque key alias.

fields.name: designValues
fields.note: Fy, Fu, E = 29,000 ksi (const, ×1000→psi at boundary). AISC shapes database supplies A/S/I/Z per section (SectionProperties.Z populated).

fields.name: specKeys
fields.note: shape (W/HSS/C/L) | designation (e.g. W12x26) | grade (A992/A500).

fields.name: factorIds
fields.note: phi(0.90 flex / 0.90 shear) | omega | Cb (LTB modification) — emitted as ModificationFactor (phi with limitState).

fields.name: limitStatesExtra
fields.note: LTB, flangeLocalBuckling, webLocalBuckling, compactness — plastic vs elastic section classification.

fields.note: gross | plastic — uses Zx (plastic modulus, SectionProperties.Z) for compact sections; gross/elastic otherwise.

fields.name: connectionMethod
fields.note: boltYield | weld | boltBearing.

fields.note: AISC beam/column quick-selection (load tables) — stubbed.

fields.name: philosophy
fields.note: LRFD · max factored (ASD permitted).

fields.name: stockForm
fields.note: linear → cut list + offcut pool (mill lengths, cut to span).

types.notes: Stub. Structurally identical interface surface to the others — the only material-specific complication is plastic-section design (Zx, handled by SectionProperties.Z) and a richer LTB/compactness check set, both via limitStatesExtra + sectionBasis without touching the core.

types.desc: Masonry leaf (CMU/brick, reinforced and unreinforced). Stubbed but structurally complete. The fifth material in the user's forward list; included so the implementations enum and the leaf set are exhaustive. Like concrete, it departs from linear-cut assumptions (unit-based laying), proving the stockForm routing generalizes to the fourth value (unit).

fields.name: id
fields.note: 'TMS-402-16'. Opaque key alias.

fields.name: designValues
fields.note: f′m (masonry strength, psi), Em, grout f′g, rebar fy. designValuesRef null.

fields.name: specKeys
fields.note: unit(CMU/brick) | nominalSize(8/10/12in) | grouting(solid/partial) | mortarType(M/S/N).

fields.name: factorIds
fields.note: phi | slendernessReduction | groutedAreaRatio — emitted as ModificationFactor (reinforced/unreinforced is a sectionBasis/spec discriminator, not a factor).

fields.name: limitStatesExtra
fields.note: axialSlenderness, flexuralTension, shearFriction, anchorBolt.

fields.name: sectionBasis
fields.note: net (mortared net section) | transformed (reinforced).

fields.name: connectionMethod
fields.note: anchorBolt | rebarDevelopment | dowel.
