fields.note: TMS empirical/prescriptive design tables — stubbed.

fields.name: philosophy
fields.note: ASD (allowable stress) or strength design — TMS supports both; mode-flag driven.

fields.name: stockForm
fields.note: unit → unit count + grout/mortar volume (block + mortar + grout takeoff), NOT a cut list. The fourth routing value (linear|sheet|cast|unit), confirming routing is by stockForm not material.

types.notes: Stub. stockForm=unit is the fourth output path (alongside linear/sheet/cast) — confirms the routing enum, not material branching in the core, is what generalizes. Demonstrates the seam scales to a material that is neither cut nor cast but laid. (Corrected from the draft: reinforced is a spec/basis discriminator, not a 'reinforcedFlag' factor.)

layers.purpose: The 1D cutting-stock / offcut tracer. Every required cut becomes a Demand line; the CuttingStockSolver assigns demand to real buyable StockOptions drawn ONLY from the supplier catalog, kerf-aware, reusing remainders from the OffcutPool by smart-match. Couples 'which sticks to buy' and 'how to cut them' to minimize waste then cost. Emits a CutPlan aggregate (CutAssignment[] per consumed stick + de-duplicated buy-list + derived waste/quantity/cost rollups) plus per-cut PieceProvenance that back-links each cut to the canonical materials.Piece, the producing CutAssignment, and the satisfied Demand. The CutPlan is the single bottom-up handoff to the estimating layer. Capability is gated to stockForm=linear ONLY via CutEligibility: sheet goods route to nesting, cast routes to formwork+volume, unit stock bypasses entirely (design-standard gotcha #3).
layers.baseUnitCompliance: All linear quantities (cut length, stock length, kerf, endTrim, offcut/remainder length, minReusable, totalWaste) are integer ticks @ 1/32in; 1in = 32 ticks, 96in = 3072 ticks, 92.625in = 2964 ticks. Catalog lengths are read THROUGH SupplierSku.stockLength (already ticks in materials), so this layer stores no inch-annotated length; the misleading inch examples on the old solver.StockOption.length note are dropped (S3-B). All money is DERIVED through the PriceQuote reference (float USD with bulkTiers + asOf), never inline cents (S3-C). Waste fraction, utilization, the material quantity rollup, and rolled cost are DERIVED real; no area appears in this layer and nothing is typed ticks² (S2-B area-free, S2-D). This is a strictly 1D problem along a single stick axis, so no TickVec3 (position) vs UnitVec3 (direction) types are needed or used — their absence is correct, not an omission.
layers.extensibilityNotes: Material-agnostic by construction. Demand.specRef and StockOption.specRef point at design-standard.StockSpec (the renamed, material-discriminated flyweight, S2-C) and design-standard's stockForm; neither names 'wood'. The CutEligibility guard (stockForm=linear) is the only seam this layer touches: cold-formed-steel studs and rebar are also stockForm=linear and flow through CuttingStockSolver UNCHANGED; sheet WSP/CFS sheet (stockForm=sheet) and concrete (stockForm=cast) are routed away to their own output paths, so adding masonry or hot-rolled later only adds a stockForm classification behind the DesignStandard Strategy seam — never a new branch in this solver. New cost models (bulk tiers, pack-rounding, freight) plug in entirely behind PriceQuote without touching cut logic. CutPlan deliberately carries NO wood-specific 'board-feet' field; the material-quantity rollup is a generic DerivedQuantity whose unit is named by the material's StockSpec.quantityBasis (board-ft for wood, lb or lineal-ft for steel), so a new material contributes a quantity unit, not a schema edit. StockOption stores no intrinsic SKU data (length/pack/price live on SupplierSku/PriceQuote and are read by reference), so adding SKU attributes never touches this layer.
resolvesFindings: S3-C (StockOption/SupplierSku inline cents → reference PriceQuote float USD)
S2-C (LumberSpec/LumberStock → StockSpec/Stock + material discriminator; specRef is material-agnostic)
S2-D (no ticks²; area-free layer; inch annotations dropped)
S2-B (geometry/area kept out of this layer; quantities derived real)
S3-A (former dangling Piece.parentStock/fulfills now typed via PieceProvenance → CutAssignment + Demand)
S3-B (uniform tick vocabulary; lengths read through SupplierSku.stockLength)
S6-flyweight (StockOption carries no copied intrinsic SKU data — contextual selection only)
gotcha#3 (cut optimization typed as a stockForm=linear capability via CutEligibility; sheet/cast/unit routed away)

types.desc: One required cut: a final cut length the framing needs, with multiplicity, role, spec, and provenance back to the member that demanded it. The COMPLETE Demand[] is built before cutting begins — greedy as-you-go packs worse than whole-picture optimization.

fields.name: lineKey
fields.note: Stable line-key (NOT entity identity — Demand is immutable; identity lives on sourcePlacement). Lets CutAssignment.cuts[].demandRef and PieceProvenance.fulfills reference this line. Was 'id' in the draft; renamed to signal it keys a line, not an entity.

fields.name: length
fields.note: Final cut length in 1/32in ticks. NOT inches — 92.625in = 2964 ticks.

fields.name: qty
fields.note: How many identical cuts of this length+role+spec are needed.

fields.name: role
fields.note: stud | plate | header | cripple | sill | block | ... — selects min-reusable rules and groups the takeoff.

fields.name: specRef
fields.note: → materials-stock.StockSpec flyweight (material-agnostic; was LumberSpec, S2-C). The spec a fulfilling stock must satisfy: species/grade/section for wood, gauge/section for CFS, bar size for rebar.

fields.name: sourcePlacement
fields.note: → placement-layer MemberPlacement. The member instance this cut serves — carries cost back to a location for top-down estimate validation. This is where the demand's real identity lives.

fields.name: endCut
fields.note: square | miter | bevel | birdsmouth — the cut geometry at the ends; affects effective length / saw waste at the cut, not the buy decision. Default square. (Renamed from 'grainOrCut' — there is no grain in this 1D layer; the field was always about the end-cut.)

types.notes: Immutable value line item. lineKey is content-addressable, not an entity id — identity belongs to the source member. Source solver.Demand had no id at all; the line-key is added (not invented identity) purely so downstream cuts/pieces can cite the exact line.

types.desc: The stockForm verdict that decides whether a Demand may enter this optimizer at all. Cut optimization + offcut reuse is a capability of stockForm=linear ONLY (design-standard gotcha #3). Sheet → nesting, cast → formwork+volume, unit → bypass. A typed gate, not an implicit assumption.

fields.name: stockForm
fields.note: linear | sheet | cast | unit — read from StockSpec.stockForm (the DesignStandard seam owns the value). ONLY 'linear' is eligible here.

fields.name: eligible
fields.note: Derived: stockForm === 'linear'. False routes the demand away from this layer entirely.

fields.name: routeTo
fields.note: When ineligible names the downstream output path: nest (sheet) | formworkVolume (cast) | direct (unit). Null when eligible.

crossRefs: design-standard.DesignStandard (Strategy seam owns the form→output mapping)
types.notes: Resolves gotcha #3 as a typed verdict consumed by CuttingStockSolver. Adding hot-rolled (linear) or masonry (unit/cast) later only sets stockForm upstream; this guard and the solver never grow a branch. The form→route mapping is owned by the seam, not duplicated here — this VO only records the verdict for a given demand.

types.desc: A buyable choice the solver may pick, drawn ONLY from the supplier catalog — the constraint set, no imaginary stock. A thin CONTEXTUAL selection wrapper: it names which catalog SKU satisfies which spec and whether it is currently buyable; it stores NO intrinsic SKU data (length, pack, price, supplier) — those are read through skuRef so the flyweight is never copied.

fields.name: skuRef
fields.note: → materials-stock.SupplierSku flyweight. Source of truth for usable length (stockLength, ticks), packSize, unitOfSale, supplier, availability, asOf. The solver reads length/pack THROUGH this ref — it is not copied onto the option (flyweight discipline, S6).

fields.name: specRef
fields.note: → materials-stock.StockSpec this option satisfies. Must equal Demand.specRef for assignment. The one genuinely contextual match-fact this VO adds.

fields.name: buyable
fields.note: Derived gate from SupplierSku.availability/inStock at solve time: an out-of-stock SKU cannot be an option. Was silently assumed in the source ('no imaginary stock'); typed here so stale/zero inventory excludes the option (source stressed asOf staleness).

crossRefs: materials.SupplierSku (skuRef — length, packSize, unitOfSale, availability, asOf, price all read here, S6)
materials.PriceQuote (reached via SupplierSku.price — S3-C, never inline cents)
types.notes: Was solver.StockOption with inline length-ticks + price:cents + packSize. The inline price:cents is replaced by the SupplierSku→PriceQuote reference (S3-C). The copied length/packSize are removed — they are intrinsic to the SKU flyweight and now read by reference (S6 flyweight fix). What remains is purely contextual: which spec this SKU fills and whether it is buyable now.

types.desc: One reusable remainder produced by a prior cut. Promoted from the inline {length, parentRef} shape in solver.OffcutPool.pieces to a named value object so the pool, provenance, and cost-origin chain can all reference it.

fields.name: id
fields.note: Stable id so a later CutAssignment can declare it consumed this offcut (sourceOffcutRef).

fields.name: length
fields.note: Remaining usable length in ticks.

fields.name: specRef
fields.note: → materials-stock.StockSpec — an offcut can only fulfill demand of the same spec.

fields.name: parentAssignmentRef
fields.note: → CutAssignment.id that produced this remainder. The provenance back-link.

fields.name: originSkuRef
fields.note: → materials-stock.SupplierSku of the originally-bought stick at the root of this offcut's chain. REQUIRED (raised from optional): every offcut traces to exactly one purchased stick, so reuse always carries a costable origin and the estimating layer can prove the cost was already booked. Resolved transitively when the parent assignment was itself an offcut reuse.

types.notes: Zero-marginal-cost supply once produced: its cost was booked to the origin stick, so reuse reduces the estimate. originSkuRef made required so the bottom-up takeoff never has an uncostable orphan offcut.

types.desc: The running, mutable set of reusable remainders. Checked BEFORE opening new stock so leftovers become supply. Identity persists across the whole solve (and optionally across a project), accumulating and consuming offcuts. The only entity in the layer — statefulness is the point.

fields.name: id
fields.note: Identity persists — the pool is stateful, not a snapshot.

fields.name: pieces
fields.note: Current reusable remainders (was inline {length,parentRef}[] — now typed Offcut, S3-A).

fields.name: minReusable
fields.note: Reuse threshold ≈ clear bay span (14.5in @ 16in OC = 464 ticks). Below this an offcut is kept only if a shorter demand (e.g. cripple) still fits; otherwise it is waste.

fields.name: matchMode
fields.note: smart | exact | none — 'smart' fits any offcut to any remaining same-spec demand. Default smart.

fields.name: scope
fields.note: solve | project — whether the pool resets per solve or persists across a project's runs. Default solve. 'project' is what lets yesterday's scrap supply today's cut.

types.notes: The sole entity here; everything else is a value object, a re-carded reference, or a service. The CuttingStockSolver consults and mutates it in/out.

types.desc: The material loss per cut, counted between adjacent cuts on a stick. Extracted from the bare solver 'kerf' field so it is named, reusable, and tool-derivable.

fields.name: kerf
fields.note: Blade width consumed per cut ≈ 1/8in = 4 ticks. Counted once per interior cut on a stick.

fields.name: endTrim
fields.note: Squaring/defect trim removed from each new stick before the first cut. Default 0.

fields.name: toolRef
fields.note: → workspace-render.ToolDefinition flyweight when kerf is tool-derived (saw blade). Keeps the intrinsic blade width out of per-demand data — one KerfSpec serves the whole solve.

types.notes: Intrinsic to the cutting tool, not the demand — correctly factored out. Reads the blade width through the ToolDefinition flyweight rather than copying it.

types.desc: The optimizer's tunable goal: the user dial between minimizing waste and minimizing cost, plus the solve method. Extracted from the inline solver objective/method strings so estimating can record what was optimized for and reproduce it.

fields.name: primary
fields.note: minWaste | minCost — the dominant objective.

fields.name: costWeight
fields.note: 0.0–1.0 blend (0 = pure waste, 1 = pure cost). The 'user dial'. Default 0 (waste-first, cost as tiebreak).

fields.name: method
fields.note: ilpOptimal (small N) | ffdPlusPool (first-fit-decreasing + offcut pool) | auto. Default auto by demand count. Recorded on the solve for reproducibility.

types.notes: Pure value — captured on the plan so the resulting estimate is reproducible and explainable ('optimized for cost at weight 0.3 via ffdPlusPool').

types.desc: The coupled 1D cutting-stock solver. Takes the full Demand[] (pre-filtered by CutEligibility) + the catalog StockOption[] + OffcutPool + KerfSpec + CutObjective and chooses stock mix AND cut pattern together. Consults the OffcutPool before opening new stock. Only same-spec, stockForm=linear, buyable demand enters; material-blind throughout.

fields.name: demand
fields.note: in — the COMPLETE demand list, already filtered to CutEligibility.eligible. Partial lists pack worse.

fields.name: options
fields.note: in — buyable constraint set from the supplier layer (each with buyable=true). The solver may use nothing outside this set.

fields.name: pool
fields.note: in/out — consulted first, mutated as offcuts are produced and consumed.

fields.name: kerf
fields.note: in — per-cut loss, counted into every interior cut plus endTrim per new stick.

fields.name: objective
fields.note: in — min-waste-then-cost dial + method.

fields.name: eligibility
fields.note: in — REQUIRED guard (raised from optional): the solver must verify each demand's stockForm=linear verdict and reject/route ineligible demand (gotcha #3). Without this gate the gotcha is only an assumption again.

fields.name: plan
fields.note: out — the assignment bundle: CutAssignment[], buy-list, and derived waste/quantity/cost rollups for estimating.

types.notes: Two coupled problems — which sticks to buy and how to cut them — solved together; that coupling is what minimizes waste. Stateless verb operating over the stateful OffcutPool. Material-blind: never inspects species/gauge, only spec-match + length + kerf + eligibility verdict.

types.desc: One stock stick's plan: the ordered cuts taken from it, kerf between them, and the fate of the final remainder. The provenance row — this stick (bought or reused) → these cuts → this leftover → reused or scrapped.

fields.name: id
fields.note: Stable id; referenced by Offcut.parentAssignmentRef and PieceProvenance.producedBy.

fields.name: stockRef
fields.note: → materials-stock.SupplierSku via the chosen StockOption — the bought stick. Null when sourceOffcutRef is set. Exactly one of stockRef / sourceOffcutRef is set (the buy-vs-reuse discriminator).

fields.name: sourceOffcutRef
fields.note: → Offcut.id consumed instead of new stock. Exactly one of stockRef / sourceOffcutRef is set — distinguishes a real purchase from a zero-cost reuse for the estimate.

fields.name: cuts
fields.note: Ordered cuts; order matters for kerf/remainder. CutLine is a tiny inline VO {demandRef: uuid (→ Demand.lineKey), length: int ticks} — no identity, no cross-schema reuse, so kept inline rather than top-level.

fields.name: kerfTotal
fields.note: Σ kerf over interior cuts + endTrim. Derived; stored for audit.

fields.name: remainder
fields.note: (stockLength read via stockRef/sourceOffcutRef) − Σ cut lengths − kerfTotal. The leftover, in ticks.

fields.name: remainderFate
fields.note: pooled | waste — pooled (≥ minReusable or fits a short demand) re-enters OffcutPool as a new Offcut; waste is scrap. Σ fates across the plan = exact buy count + exact waste.

fields.name: utilization
fields.note: Derived real: Σ cut lengths / stockLength. Renamed from 'yield' (a JS/TS reserved word). Utilization for the waste report — a ratio, NOT a stored linear quantity.

crossRefs: materials.SupplierSku (stockRef — and stockLength read by ref for remainder)
Offcut (sourceOffcutRef consumed; produces a new Offcut when remainderFate=pooled)
types.notes: stockLength is read through stockRef/sourceOffcutRef, never copied onto the assignment (flyweight discipline). The stockRef-xor-sourceOffcutRef discriminator is what lets the estimating layer count only real purchases and treat reuses as zero marginal cost.

types.desc: ↳ Defined in **materials-stock**. Referenced from this stage. The cut-result entity. Its canonical home and full definition (id, parentMemberId, cutIds,

The cut-layer's per-cut provenance record, owned HERE (not on materials.Piece). Links one canonical materials.Piece to the CutAssignment that produced it and the Demand line it satisfied, and flags reuse. This is the type that resolves the former dangling solver.Piece.parentStock/fulfills (S3-A) without bloating the materials entity.

fields.name: pieceRef
fields.note: → materials-stock.Piece.id — the canonical cut result this record annotates. Single-sourced; never restates Piece fields.

fields.name: producedBy
fields.note: → CutAssignment.id this piece came from. The stick→cut link (resolves the dangling materials-stock.Piece.parentStock, S3-A).

fields.name: fulfills
fields.note: → Demand.lineKey satisfied (resolves the dangling materials-stock.Piece.fulfills, S3-A).

fields.name: isOffcutReuse
fields.note: True if cut from a reused Offcut rather than newly-bought stock — zero marginal material cost for the estimate. Mirrors CutAssignment's sourceOffcutRef branch.

crossRefs: estimating layer (walks pieceRef ← producedBy ← stockRef bottom-up)
types.notes: Single-source fix for S3-A: rather than re-defining materials.Piece with cut-specific fields, the cut layer owns this thin provenance VO. The provenance chain Stock/Offcut → CutAssignment → PieceProvenance → materials.Piece is what the estimating layer walks bottom-up to attribute cost back to a MemberPlacement.

types.desc: The solver's aggregate output bundle: every CutAssignment, the de-duplicated pack-rounded buy-list, and the DERIVED waste / material-quantity / cost rollups. The single handoff object the estimating layer consumes for bottom-up takeoff. A genuinely-missing type — the original solver emitted a bare CutAssignment[] with no aggregate, but the estimating requirement needs one costed, quantity-rolled, buy-listed bundle.

fields.name: id
fields.note: Identity of this solve result (versionable snapshot for Neon/Drizzle).

fields.name: assignments
fields.note: Every stick plan, buys and reuses both.

fields.name: buyList
fields.note: De-duplicated purchases only (reuses excluded — cost already booked). BuyLine is an inline VO {skuRef: SkuKey, count: int, packsRoundedTo: int} — no identity, no reuse. Feeds estimating + the supplier order.

fields.name: totalWaste
fields.note: Σ remainder where remainderFate=waste. Linear, so ticks — the only summed length in the aggregate.

fields.name: wasteFraction
fields.note: Derived real: totalWaste / Σ bought stockLength. The headline waste % — a ratio, never ticks.

fields.name: materialQuantity
fields.note: Generic derived material rollup for the estimate: { value: float, unit: string }. Unit is named by the spec's StockSpec.quantityBasis — board-ft for wood, lb or lineal-ft for steel, etc. Replaces the draft's wood-specific 'boardFeet' (an extensibility leak): a new material contributes a quantity unit, not a schema edit. Derived real — never ticks, never ticks².

fields.name: rolledCost
fields.note: Derived through each BuyLine's SupplierSku → PriceQuote (bulkTiers + pack rounding). Float USD, never cents (S3-C). The bottom-up material cost the estimating layer validates top-down. [money = real decimal USD, single-sourced via PriceQuote — no cents]

fields.name: objective
fields.note: What this plan optimized for — recorded for reproducibility.

crossRefs: estimating layer (primary consumer of the bottom-up takeoff)
types.notes: Genuinely-missing aggregate type. BuyLine and DerivedQuantity are inline VOs (no identity, no cross-schema reuse). All money/quantity/fraction fields are derived real per the base-unit invariant; only totalWaste (a length) stays in ticks. The former 'boardFeet' field is generalized to materialQuantity so the aggregate stays material-agnostic for CFS/concrete/masonry/hot-rolled.

layers.purpose: Bottom-up project estimating: turn the solved domain graph (Piece / MemberPlacement / Connection cut list + OffcutPool waste + kerf loss) and the supplier catalog (SupplierSku / PriceQuote flyweights, owned by materials) into a fully cost-coded, marked-up project Estimate, with every dollar traceable back to the domain object that demanded it and validated top-down against an external benchmark (RSMeans assembly, ENR, historical). Material-agnostic: priced via Stock.stockForm (linear stick → cut list / sheet → nest / cast → formwork+volume / unit → prefab assembly count), so wood today and CFS / concrete / masonry / hot-rolled later flow through the same line-item machinery without core edits. Implements forward requirement (2). Money is real decimal USD; linear takeoff quantities are sourced from canonical int-tick fields and converted to a unit-of-measure exactly once, retaining the source ticks for audit.
layers.baseUnitCompliance: Linear takeoff quantities are sourced from canonical int-tick fields (Demand.length, CutAssignment.cuts/remainder, Piece.length, OffcutPool remainders, CutAssignment.kerf) and stored on TakeoffItem as { quantity:real, uom } — the int-tick magnitude is converted to the line's unit-of-measure (LF, BF, EA, SF, CY, LB) exactly once at takeoff time via UnitOfMeasure, and the source tick value is retained in TakeoffItem.sourceTicks for round-trip audit. No area/volume is ever typed ticks² or ticks³: board-feet, square-feet, cubic-yards are real, consistent with materials' rule that area/volume/weight/price are derived real (and the counter-example to solver's Face.area ticks² flagged in S2-D). All money fields (unitCost, extendedCost, rate, amount, total, nodeTotal, deltaTotal) are decimal real USD, never int cents. TickVec3 / UnitVec3 are not used in this layer — it carries no geometric positions or directions, only scalar quantities and money; geometry stays upstream in the kernel/domain layers and reaches estimating only as already-resolved tick magnitudes on TakeoffItem.sourceTicks. UnitOfMeasure is the single explicit seam between the int-tick world and the real-decimal cost world; ticksPerUnit is the only conversion factor and it lives in exactly one place.
layers.extensibilityNotes: Open for new materials WITHOUT core edits: (1) MaterialLine prices off Stock.stockForm, NOT species — a CFS stud, a rebar stick, a sheet of OSB, a cubic yard of concrete, and a prefab joist hanger are all a CostType=material line with a different uom and SupplierSku; the pricing path (linear→cut list/offcut waste, sheet→nest yield, cast→formwork+volume, unit→prefab count) is selected by the carried stockForm discriminator exactly as the design-standard caveat requires (cast concrete bypasses the cut optimizer; prefab hardware is a unit count off the connection graph). stockForm is REFERENCED from Stock, not redeclared as an owned enum, so adding a stockForm value is a Stock-layer edit, never an estimating edit. (2) AssemblyCost is a flyweight reference catalog keyed like LumberSpec/SupplierSku/ToolDefinition — new RSMeans assemblies (steel-stud-partition, CMU-wall, slab-on-grade) are catalog rows, never new types. (3) CostCode is a reference flyweight over a pluggable classification (MasterFormat | Uniformat | custom) via codeSystem discriminator, so a steel/concrete trade adds Division rows. (4) ResourceRate covers labor|equipment generically with a discipline tag, so steel erection crews or concrete pump rates drop in as data. (5) CostType is a closed economic enum (material|labor|equipment|subcontract|overhead) that is material-blind by construction. New cost models (productivity vs unit-price vs lump-sum) are AssemblyCost.method variants — data, not types. The top-down validator (CostBenchmark) is an «interface» Strategy seam — the cost-side mirror of design-standard.DesignStandard: an RSMeans adapter is one leaf; ENR / supplier-index / historical-job adapters plug in behind it with zero core edits.
resolvesFindings: S3-C (price-representation drift): PriceQuote stays single-sourced in MATERIALS (its canonical home per the registry — SupplierSku.price references it); this layer references PriceQuote read-only and forbids int-cents. The fix is to make solver.StockOption.price reference materials.PriceQuote — NOT to relocate PriceQuote into estimating (that would re-break single-source the other way). The draft's relocation is corrected here.

S3-A (dangling ref PriceTier): promoted from the inline {minQty,pricePer} shape to a named value object, but homed in MATERIALS alongside PriceQuote (it is an attribute of the material price snapshot, not an estimating concept). Estimating references it via MaterialLine.appliedTier for audit.
S2-C (material discriminator): consumes Stock/StockSpec + stockForm rather than wood-specific LumberStock, keeping estimating material-agnostic; stockForm is referenced from Stock, not redeclared.

NEW (absent estimating pipeline stage): no source artifact owned cost rollup; this layer adds bottom-up takeoff from the connection graph + cut list + supplier SKUs into a project Estimate, validated top-down (forward requirement 2).

Reinforces flyweight discipline (S1/DRY): AssemblyCost, CostCode, ResourceRate are shared reference catalogs looked up by key, never copied per line — same pattern as LumberSpec/SupplierSku/ToolDefinition.

Reinforces base-unit invariant (S2-D): UnitOfMeasure is the single tick→cost-quantity conversion seam; no ticks²/ticks³ anywhere; kerf and offcut waste are quantified from real CutAssignment data, not guessed.

Reinforces incremental-recompute (architecture RunningTotal): CostRollup produces RollupNode values consumed by Estimate; 'cost' and 'weight' are named RunningTotals kept live by the Rust worker on the same dirty-propagation graph.

types.desc: The five canonical economic categories every cost line resolves to. Material-blind, closed enum — the axis along which an estimate is summarized and along which subcontractor scope is carved out. Standard estimating vocabulary (RSMeans / construction cost accounting).

fields.name: kind
fields.note: material | labor | equipment | subcontract | overhead. Exhaustive; new materials never add a kind.

fields.name: isMarkupBase
fields.note: Whether this category is eligible to carry markup (e.g. subcontract often gets a lower markup than self-perform material+labor).

types.notes: A value enum, not a flyweight — identity-free and intrinsically tiny. CostCode partitions WHERE the dollar lands (WBS); CostType partitions WHAT KIND of dollar it is.

types.desc: The unit a quantity is expressed and priced in, and the SINGLE seam between the int-tick geometry world and the real-decimal cost world. Carries the conversion factor from canonical ticks where the unit is linear; non-linear units (BF/SF/CY/LB) are derived-real and carry no tick exponent.

fields.name: code
fields.note: EA | LF | BF | SF | SY | CY | LB | TON | HR | DAY | LS. (each, linear-ft, board-ft, square-ft, square-yd, cubic-yd, pound, ton, hour, day, lump-sum.) [open registry keyed by dimension; new unit = data row, not enum edit]

fields.name: dimension
fields.note: count | length | area | volume | weight | time | lumpsum — tells the rollup how to aggregate and which derived-real rule applies.

fields.name: ticksPerUnit
fields.note: Conversion from canonical int ticks to this unit ONLY for dimension=length (LF = 384 ticks/ft). Null for all non-linear dimensions — BF/SF/CY are computed from section geometry, LB/TON from density; never tick-squared/cubed. Keeps the tick→cost-qty conversion in exactly one place.

types.notes: This is the type that guarantees no field is ever typed ticks²: a tick magnitude enters here (length only) and a real quantity leaves; area/volume/weight enter already-derived as real from the domain layers.

types.desc: A shared flyweight classification node (the work-breakdown coordinate a cost lands on) over a pluggable coding system. Looked up by key, never copied per line — same pattern as LumberSpec/SupplierSku. The estimate's columnar structure (Division → section → pay-item) comes from these.

fields.name: key
fields.note: Opaque catalog key, e.g. MF-06-11-00 (Wood Framing) or UF-B1010 (Floor Construction). Intrinsic flyweight key.

fields.name: codeSystem
fields.note: masterformat | uniformat | custom. Discriminator that lets steel/concrete/masonry trades add divisions without new types.

fields.name: code
fields.note: As-published code, e.g. '06 11 00' (MasterFormat) or 'B1010' (Uniformat).

fields.name: title
fields.note: Human label, e.g. 'Wood Framing', 'Cast-in-Place Concrete'.

fields.name: parentKey
fields.note: Hierarchy edge → Division/section roll-up; null at root. Drives the CostRollup tree.

fields.name: level
fields.note: Depth in the WBS (0=division). Convenience for grouped display.

A shared flyweight unit rate for a productive resource — a labor trade crew or a piece of equipment — looked up by key. The INTRINSIC published cost-per-unit; the contextual quantity (and any per-estimate burden/region override) lives on the line that references it. fields.name: key
fields.note: Intrinsic flyweight key, e.g. LAB-CARP-JOUR, EQP-NAILGUN-PNEU.

fields.note: Almost always HR; equipment may be HR or DAY.

fields.note: Published fringe/burden/tax multiplier (e.g. 1.35) for the fully-loaded rate. Intrinsic default; an estimate may OVERRIDE it contextually on the consuming line, so bare vs burdened stays auditable and single-sourced.

fields.name: region
fields.note: Published labor-market / city-cost-index region the bare rate is keyed to (intrinsic to the published row). Per-estimate regional adjustment is applied at consumption, not by mutating this row.

types.desc: The traceability atom of bottom-up estimating: one measured quantity tied back to the exact domain object that demanded it (Piece / MemberPlacement / Connection edge / CutAssignment / OffcutPool waste / kerf loss). Every dollar in the estimate descends from a TakeoffItem, so any cost can be drilled to its source geometry. This is what makes the takeoff auditable rather than a flat spreadsheet.

fields.name: id
fields.note: Stable identity for audit/round-trip.

fields.name: sourceKind
fields.note: piece | memberPlacement | connection | cutAssignment | offcutWaste | kerfWaste | assembly | manual — what kind of domain object this measures. 'connection' covers fastener/hardware takeoff off the connection graph; 'kerfWaste' captures saw-kerf consumed material.

fields.name: sourceRef
fields.note: Typed pointer to the actual source: Piece.id | MemberPlacement.id | Connection edge id | CutAssignment id | OffcutPool entry. The traceability link back into the connection graph + cut list.

fields.name: quantity
fields.note: Measured quantity in the line's UOM. Derived-real (LF/BF/SF/CY/EA/LB), converted from canonical ticks via UnitOfMeasure exactly once.

fields.name: uom
fields.note: Unit of measure for quantity; carries the tick conversion for linear dimensions.

fields.name: sourceTicks
fields.note: Original canonical int-tick magnitude for linear takeoffs (Demand/Piece/CutAssignment length, kerf, remainder), retained for round-trip audit and to prove no precision was lost. Null for count/area/volume/weight sources, which arrive already-derived-real from the domain.

fields.name: wasteFlag
fields.note: True when sourceKind=offcutWaste or kerfWaste — lets the rollup separate paid-for waste (remainderFate=waste from CutAssignment, plus kerf) from installed quantity for value engineering.

fields.name: costCodeKey
fields.note: → CostCode the quantity rolls up under (WBS coordinate).

fields.name: note
fields.note: e.g. 'king studs, gable wall' — human breadcrumb.

crossRefs: solver: MemberPlacement, Demand, CutAssignment (remainder + kerf), OffcutPool (sourceRef + tick quantities)
estimating-cost: MaterialLine / ResourceLine / AssemblyCost (consume TakeoffItem quantities)
types.notes: The bottom-up keystone. A change in geometry → re-solve → new CutAssignment → new TakeoffItem → recomputed cost, all in the same dirty-propagation graph the Rust worker runs. sourceTicks + uom together honor the tick invariant at the cost boundary; kerfWaste closes the saw-loss gap the draft omitted.

types.desc: A single MATERIAL cost line: a takeoff quantity priced against a SupplierSku/PriceQuote, with break-tier selection, packaging rounding, and waste applied. Material-agnostic — works for a stick of lumber, a sheet of OSB, rebar, a cubic yard of concrete, or a box of prefab hangers via the SKU's UOM and the carried Stock.stockForm pricing path. Labor/equipment cost lines are a SEPARATE type (ResourceLine) — this keeps the material flyweight (SKU/PriceQuote) cleanly distinct from the labor flyweight (ResourceRate).

fields.name: takeoffRef
fields.note: → TakeoffItem.id — the measured quantity and its source.

fields.name: skuRef
fields.note: → materials-stock.SupplierSku — the buyable product, its unitOfSale and packSize.

fields.name: priceRef
fields.note: → the materials-stock.PriceQuote snapshot used (resolved via the SKU). Real USD. References materials' single money source; never inlines a price.

fields.name: stockFormRef
fields.note: linear | sheet | cast | unit — CARRIED (referenced) from Stock.stockForm, not redeclared here. Selects pricing path: linear→exact cut-list waste from CutAssignment/OffcutPool; sheet→nest yield; cast→volume+formwork; unit→prefab count. Adding a stockForm value is a Stock-layer edit, not an estimating edit.

fields.name: netQty
fields.note: Installed (net) quantity before waste/rounding, from the TakeoffItem. Derived-real.

fields.name: buyQty
fields.note: Purchase quantity AFTER waste allowance and packaging rounding (SupplierSku.unitOfSale / packSize) — what actually gets ordered. Derived-real.

fields.name: appliedTier
fields.note: Which break tier buyQty landed in (audit). References the materials PriceTier VO.

fields.name: wasteFactor
fields.note: Fractional allowance over net (e.g. 0.10) used ONLY for sheet/cast/unit or when no cut-list exists. For stockForm=linear, buyQty derives from EXACT CutAssignment remainder+kerf via OffcutPool, not this blanket factor.

fields.name: unitCost
fields.note: Effective unit cost after tier selection. Real USD.

fields.name: extendedCost
fields.note: buyQty × unitCost. Derived-real; never cents. [money = real decimal USD, single-sourced via PriceQuote — no cents]

crossRefs: solver: CutAssignment (remainder + kerf), OffcutPool (exact waste source for stockForm=linear)

Splitting ResourceLine out fixes the draft, which overloaded labor onto the material line.

types.desc: A single LABOR or EQUIPMENT cost line: a quantity of resource-time priced against a ResourceRate flyweight, with any per-estimate burden/region override applied here (contextually) rather than mutating the shared rate row. fields.name: costType

layers.purpose: Formalizes architecture.html (prose+diagram, no MODEL object) into a machine-readable type registry. Encodes the frontend/worker compute-locality contract: a Rust/WASM worker owns the canonical SoA tick arrays + dependency-graph recompute; the JS main thread owns a read-only render mirror + ToolRunner. The two communicate over exactly two channels — wasm-bindgen Commands (Channel A, low-volume intents) and shared linear memory (Channel B, zero-copy state). The keystone is BufferLayout: byte offsets GENERATED from the single domain MODEL source, so the Rust writer and JS reader provably cannot drift. This layer is also the DECLARED CANONICAL HOME for the render-adapter types misplaced in materials (RenderMesh/MeshInstance/MaterialRef, S2-A) — single-sourced here as their own cards, not as inlined fields. Adds material-agnostic Persistence (Neon/Postgres + Drizzle snapshots, ModelVersion, R2 blobs) orthogonal to the domain. This layer describes HOW the domain runs, never WHAT it models — keeping it its own artifact avoids repeating the render-in-materials misplacement (S2-A).
layers.baseUnitCompliance: This layer holds little domain geometry of its own — it is mostly the substrate the tick invariant runs on. It enforces the invariant STRUCTURALLY rather than restating it, and where it DOES hold relocated geometry (the render-adapter cards) it obeys the invariant exactly. (1) BufferColumn.fieldClass is the keystone discriminator: linearTick→i32 ticks; derivedReal→f64 (area/volume/weight/price/cost); dir→f32 (a UnitVec3 direction/normal); key→u32 flyweight index; id→u64; flag→u8. It is GENERATED from the domain field's declared type, so a domain field typed vec3<int> ticks (a TickVec3 position) emits three i32 linearTick columns, while a vec3 direction (a UnitVec3 normal) emits f32 dir columns, and an area emits one f64 derivedReal column and is NEVER allocated as tick² (S2-D forbidden by construction). (2) MeshInstance.origin — the relocated mesh placement — is typed TickVec3 (vec3<int>, ticks), NOT bare vec3, closing the exact tick-vs-real drift the relocation could have re-introduced; its normals/orientation use quat/UnitVec3, never ticks. BoundingBox extents are TickVec3 ticks. (3) RunningTotal.unit is explicit and DERIVED (board-ft, lbs, USD-cents, count, yd3) — incremental aggregates are real or integer-count, never ticks². (4) DirtyRange spans are expressed in element indices + byte offsets into the typed columns, unit-neutral. No field in this layer is typed in ticks except (a) where it mirrors a domain int column (tagged fieldClass=linearTick so the generator round-trips it as i32) and (b) the explicitly-tick render-placement fields (MeshInstance.origin, BoundingBox.min/max) carried over verbatim from materials as TickVec3.
layers.extensibilityNotes: Material-agnostic by construction, which is what lets requirement (1) (new material types via the DesignStandard seam) and requirement (2) (estimating/costs) land without touching this layer's core: (a) SoABuffer is keyed by domainTypeId (a DomainTypeRef — an opaque registry handle to ANY domain type: Stock, MemberPlacement, ConnectionEdge, CutAssignment, PayItem, SupplierSku), so adding cold-formed-steel/concrete/masonry/hot-rolled types or cost/estimate types just registers new buffers; no new RustWorker/Seam/channel code. (b) BufferLayout is GENERATED from the MODEL source — a new domain type or a new field on an existing one re-emits offsets for both Rust and JS from one declaration; the Rust/JS contract can never be hand-edited out of sync. (c) RunningTotal is an open registry of named incremental aggregates ({key, unit, reducer, sourceJoin}); 'cost' and 'board-ft' and 'weight' are just rows — adding 'steel-weight-lbs' or 'concrete-yd3' or a project 'estimate-total-cents' is a data row, not a type change; and because reducers can reduce over a JOIN (cut list × SupplierSku price), requirement (2)'s bottom-up takeoff over the connection graph + cut list attaches here as data. (d) LocalityRule is a data-driven registry of verdicts keyed by operation; new operations ('estimate', 'material-switch') add rows, not branches. (e) Command.kind is an open tagged-union discriminant; 'setMaterial' and 'estimate' are new tags routed (for material) through the DesignStandard seam, never edits to the channel or worker. (f) Persistence (Snapshot/ModelVersion/PersistenceStore) serializes opaque buffer bytes + a schemaVersion, so it survives any domain-schema evolution without migration of THIS layer — snapshots are material-blind and estimate-blind blobs validated by schemaVersion. The seam, channels, and worker boundary are fixed; everything that varies per-material or per-cost-model varies as registry DATA behind domainTypeId.

types.desc: The brain. A single Rust/WASM Web Worker, off the render thread, that owns canonical state and the parametric dependency-graph recompute. In-session system of record. Deterministic, CPU-bound. Holds the geometry kernel (truck/BREP), framing solver, loads traversal, structural check, cut optimizer, the connection graph, the canonical SoA tick arrays in its linear memory, and the authoritative undo/redo inverse log.

Crossed on commit, never on per-mouse-move motion.

types.desc: Channel A — calls. Carries low-volume intents JS→Rust via wasm-bindgen ('push face N by 3½"', 'undo'), and returns the set of DirtyRanges that changed. Compile-time type safety and ergonomics win here; copy cost is negligible at one-message-per-action volume. The mistake this avoids: running the huge constant model through RPC.

types.desc: Channel B — state. The model via shared linear memory. Rust owns the SoA tick arrays in WASM memory; JS makes read-only typed-array views over the same bytes to render. Zero-copy, no marshaling, every frame. Pass by reference, never by copy.

fields.name: transport

A reference because it is an intrinsic, shared, looked-up-by-key identifier of a type — never copied per instance.

types.desc: A struct-of-arrays canonical column store for ONE domain type, resident in Rust's linear memory. Each domain field becomes a parallel typed column (e.g. Stock → length:i32[] ticks, specKey:u32[]; MemberPlacement → originX/Y/Z:i32[] ticks). Integer 1/32" tick columns are the linear truth; derived real columns (area/weight/cost) are f64. The single source of truth, written only by RustWorker.

fields.name: domainTypeId
fields.note: OPAQUE ref to ANY domain type. This is the extensibility hinge — new material/cost types just register a buffer.

fields.name: elementCount
fields.note: Number of live rows (e.g. stud count). Aggregated into a RunningTotal.

fields.name: capacity
fields.note: Allocated rows before a grow is needed.

fields.name: columns
fields.note: One entry per domain field; each carries elementType + fieldClass + byte offset from BufferLayout.

fields.name: layoutRef
fields.note: Generated offsets for this buffer's columns — Rust and JS read the identical table.

fields.name: memoryRef
fields.note: The backing SharedArrayBuffer this buffer lives inside.

fields.name: baseByteOffset
fields.note: Where this buffer starts inside the shared linear memory.

fields.name: version
fields.note: Monotonic write counter; bumped on every recompute that touches this buffer (drives dirty-only redraw).

types.notes: S2-D structurally enforced via BufferColumn.fieldClass: a column whose domain field is an area/volume is derivedReal (f64), never an i32 tick² column. Resolves forward-req (1): keyed by opaque DomainTypeRef so cold-formed-steel/concrete/cost types add buffers with zero worker edits.

types.desc: One typed column inside a SoABuffer — the memory image of a single domain field. Immutable description (the data it points at is mutable; this VO is its layout). Carries the elementType, fieldClass, and the byte stride/offset so a typed-array view can be cut over exactly this column.

fields.name: fieldName
fields.note: Domain field this column mirrors (e.g. 'length', 'originX', 'boardFeet', 'unitCostCents'). [money = real decimal USD, single-sourced via PriceQuote — no cents]

fields.name: elementType
fields.note: i32 for tick/linear columns; f64 for derived area/volume/weight/price/cost; f32 for direction components; u32 for flyweight key indices; u8 for flags/enums.

fields.name: fieldClass
fields.note: The base-unit discriminator. linearTick→i32 ticks (a TickVec3 component); derivedReal→f64 (area/vol/weight/cost); dir→f32 (a UnitVec3 component, direction/normal — NEVER ticks); key→u32 flyweight index; id→u64; flag→u8. GENERATED from the domain field's declared type.

fields.name: byteOffset
fields.note: Offset of column[0] from buffer base. GENERATED, never hand-written.

fields.name: stride
fields.note: Bytes between consecutive elements (usually elementType width for pure SoA).

fields.name: componentCount
fields.note: 1 for scalars; 3 for a TickVec3/UnitVec3 split into x/y/z; 4 for a quat. A vec3<int> tick position emits componentCount=3 linearTick(i32); a vec3 direction emits componentCount=3 dir(f32).

types.notes: fieldClass is the keystone of base-unit compliance: it forces tick fields to i32 (linearTick), directions to f32 (dir/UnitVec3), and area/cost to f64 (derivedReal) at the memory layer, ending the vec3/vec3<int> and ticks² drift (S2-D, S3-B) by construction.

types.desc: THE KEYSTONE CONTRACT. A shared flyweight: the generated table of byte offsets, element types, and field classes for every column of every SoABuffer — emitted from the ONE domain MODEL source the domain schemas already declare their fields in. Both the Rust writer and the JS reader load this identical generated table, so the two sides provably cannot drift. Looked up by (domainTypeId, schemaVersion); never hand-edited.

fields.name: layoutKey
fields.note: Opaque key = hash(domainTypeId + schemaVersion + targetAlignment). The flyweight lookup key.

fields.name: domainTypeId
fields.note: Which domain type this layout describes.

fields.name: schemaVersion
fields.note: Version of the domain MODEL this layout was generated from; mismatch with a Snapshot triggers migration, never silent reinterpret.

fields.name: columns
fields.note: The generated per-field offset/type/class table (the actual contract rows).

fields.name: elementStride
fields.note: Total bytes per logical element across all columns (for capacity math).

fields.name: alignment
fields.note: Target alignment (e.g. 4 or 8) so typed-array views are valid on both sides.

fields.name: generatedFrom
fields.note: Provenance: path/id of the MODEL source artifact + generator version. Makes the 'cannot drift' claim auditable.

fields.name: generatorHash
fields.note: Hash of the codegen output; Rust build and JS build assert equality at startup.

crossRefs: ModelVersion (snapshots record schemaVersion to bind to a layout)
ALL domain types (every domain field generates a column here)

This reference type IS that contract. Forward-req (1)+(2): new material/cost types regenerate their layout from the same MODEL, automatically keeping Rust/JS in sync.

types.desc: An immutable span of changed buffer elements returned from Rust to JS after a recompute. Lets JS do a dirty-only redraw — re-view and re-render exactly the ranges that moved, not the whole model. The 'changed buffers out' arrow of the seam.

fields.name: bufferId
fields.note: Which canonical buffer changed.

fields.name: columnName
fields.note: Optional — which column; omitted means whole-element range across all columns.

fields.name: startIndex
fields.note: First changed element index.

fields.name: endIndex
fields.note: One-past-last changed element (half-open).

fields.name: byteStart
fields.note: Resolved byte offset (startIndex × stride + column offset) for direct typed-array slicing.

fields.name: byteEnd
fields.note: Resolved end byte offset.

fields.name: kind
fields.note: appended/removed/reallocated may require JS to re-view (capacity/grow change); mutated is in-place.

fields.name: causeVersion
fields.note: SoABuffer.version after the change; JS skips ranges it already rendered.

types.notes: Spans are element/byte indices — unit-neutral, never ticks². Resolves Missing-Layer-(b)'s 'dirty ranges out' prose into a typed value. kind=reallocated added so a SharedLinearMemory grow forces JS re-view.

types.desc: An incremental aggregate maintained by RustWorker across recomputes — stud count, total board-feet, total weight, and (forward-req 2) total cost / project estimate. Recomputed incrementally on dirty propagation, not by full re-scan. An OPEN registry: each total is a {key, unit, reducer, source} row, so new aggregates (steel weight, concrete yd³, estimate total) are data, not new types. A reducer may reduce over a single column OR a JOIN (e.g. cut list × SupplierSku price) so a bottom-up takeoff total is itself a row.

fields.name: key
fields.note: Stable name: 'studCount' | 'boardFeet' | 'weightLbs' | 'estimateTotalCents' | 'steelWeightLbs' | 'concreteYd3' | ... [money = real decimal USD, single-sourced via PriceQuote — no cents]

fields.name: value
fields.note: Current aggregate value (count totals are whole but stored f64 for uniformity; money is integer cents conceptually, stored f64 only at this aggregate surface — see unit). [money = real decimal USD, single-sourced via PriceQuote — no cents]

fields.name: unit
fields.note: DERIVED unit. Never ticks² — aggregates are real/integer-count. Money carried as usdCents to dodge float-money drift (S3-C alignment: cents, not float USD). [money = real decimal USD, single-sourced via PriceQuote — no cents]

fields.name: reducer
fields.note: How the per-element contributions combine; lets the worker update incrementally per DirtyRange.

fields.name: sourceBufferId
fields.note: Primary buffer it reduces over (e.g. boardFeet reduces Stock derived-volume column; studCount = Stock.elementCount).

fields.name: sourceColumn
fields.note: Column reduced; omit for pure element counts (studCount = elementCount).

fields.name: sourceJoin
fields.note: Optional join spec for derived multi-buffer totals: {joinBufferId, joinKeyColumn, valueColumn}. estimateTotalCents = Σ over CutAssignment JOIN SupplierSku of (qty × unitCostCents) — the bottom-up takeoff over the connection graph + cut list (forward-req 2). [money = real decimal USD, single-sourced via PriceQuote — no cents]

fields.name: isDerived
fields.note: False for direct input rollups (board-ft, stud count); true for solver/cost-derived totals (estimate). Lets a top-down estimate validate only the derived totals against the bottom-up takeoff.

fields.name: lastVersion
fields.note: Max buffer version this total was last reconciled against (across source + join buffers); staleness check for incremental update.

fields.name: reviewRef
fields.note: Provenance: architecture.html critical-review card # (01..05).

types.notes: Resolves Missing-Layer-(b): the 5 review cards of architecture.html become machine-readable rows. Data-driven verdict registry — forward-reqs add 'estimate'/'materialSwitch' rows without touching Seam logic.

types.desc: A persisted, point-in-time serialization of the canonical model — the SoABuffer bytes plus the schemaVersion that interprets them, plus the maintained RunningTotals. Material-agnostic and estimate-agnostic: an opaque blob bound to a layout version, validated by schemaVersion, never reinterpreted across a layout change without migration. Backs versioning and legal-deliverable stamping.

fields.name: modelVersionId
fields.note: The logical version this snapshot materializes.

fields.name: schemaVersion
fields.note: Domain MODEL version the bytes were generated from; bound to a BufferLayout. Mismatch on load → migration, never silent reinterpret.

fields.name: wasmModuleHash
fields.note: Which RustWorker logic produced it — reproducibility of derived results (loads, checks, cuts, estimate). Critical for forward-req (2): a stamped estimate must be reproducible from the same wasm + bytes.

fields.name: bufferBlobRef
fields.note: Opaque serialized buffer bytes in R2 (large; not in Postgres rows).

fields.name: runningTotals
fields.note: Snapshotted aggregates (stud count, board-ft, weight, estimate total) for fast listing without re-materializing.

fields.name: createdBy
fields.note: User/session that committed it.

fields.name: stamped
fields.note: True if frozen as a legal deliverable (drawings export / permit set / sealed estimate).

crossRefs: drawings-export: stamped snapshots back the deliverable set
types.notes: Persistence kept orthogonal to the domain: stores opaque bytes + schemaVersion, so it survives material/cost-model evolution with no migration of THIS layer. Snapshots are material-blind and estimate-blind blobs; wasmModuleHash makes derived totals (incl. estimate) reproducible.

types.desc: A logical version node in the model's history DAG — the persistence-side counterpart to RustWorker's in-session undo log. Each committed Command advances the in-memory version; a ModelVersion is the durable, addressable record (parent linkage, author, message) that Snapshots attach to. Enables branching, diffing, and restore.

fields.name: parentId
fields.note: Previous version; null for the initial. DAG, not just a line (branches allowed).

fields.name: sequence
fields.note: Monotonic per-project ordinal.

fields.name: commandId
fields.note: The committed command that produced this version (audit trail back to the intent).

fields.name: schemaVersion
fields.note: Domain MODEL version active at commit time.

fields.name: snapshotId
fields.note: Materialized snapshot, if one was taken at this version (not every version snapshots).

fields.name: message
fields.note: Human label (e.g. 'added garage wall').

types.notes: Material-agnostic: a version is just a node + schemaVersion, indifferent to which materials or cost models the snapshot bytes contain. Resolves the architecture.html 'versioning / stamps legal deliverables' prose into a typed entity.

types.desc: An immutable handle to one large opaque blob in Cloudflare R2 — a Snapshot's serialized buffer bytes or an exported PDF. Defined here so Snapshot.bufferBlobRef and the export handoff are not dangling refs (S3-A). The bytes are opaque to persistence; only schemaVersion + BufferLayout give them meaning.

fields.name: key
fields.note: R2 object key.

fields.name: bucket
fields.note: R2 bucket name.

fields.name: contentType
fields.note: octet-stream for snapshot buffer bytes; pdf for exported deliverables (the Rust→JS drawings-export terminal).

fields.name: etag
fields.note: Integrity/version tag.

types.notes: Resolves S3-A: the draft referenced ref<R2Object> without a card. Promotes the blob handle to a typed value and gives the architecture.html 'R2 holds exported PDFs' + drawings-export terminal a concrete home.

types.desc: The backend persistence boundary — Neon (Postgres) via Drizzle for structured metadata (ModelVersion DAG, project/customer rows, RunningTotal summaries, estimate headers) and Cloudflare R2 for large opaque blobs (Snapshot buffer bytes, exported PDFs). Thin Cloudflare Workers at the edge. Orthogonal to the domain: it serializes/restores without understanding materials or cost models.

fields.name: metadataStore
fields.note: Postgres via Drizzle ORM — versions, projects, totals, estimate headers.

fields.name: blobStore
fields.note: R2 — Snapshot buffer blobs and exported PDFs (large, opaque R2Objects).

fields.name: edgeRuntime
fields.note: Thin edge layer; does NOT run the parametric engine (that is the in-browser RustWorker). Supersedes the old 'backend = system of record' framing for live editing.

fields.name: save
fields.note: Persists a snapshot blob to R2 + version row to Postgres atomically.

fields.name: load
fields.note: Restores bytes; asserts schemaVersion matches a known BufferLayout before handing to RustWorker.

fields.name: migrate
fields.type: service(in: Snapshot, fromSchemaVersion, toSchemaVersion -> out: Snapshot)
fields.note: Re-emits buffer bytes against a newer BufferLayout when schemaVersion drifts — the ONLY sanctioned path to reinterpret bytes. New material/cost schemas need only a new BufferLayout + optional migration, never a persistence-layer code change.

crossRefs: estimating: estimate headers/PayItems persist via metadataStore
drawings-export: exported PDFs land in R2 (the Rust→JS terminal handoff)
types.notes: Resolves architecture.html layer-7 'Persistence' prose into a typed service, and acknowledges the drawings-export terminal (pipeline-drift #4). Kept material-agnostic and estimate-orthogonal: it moves bytes + version metadata; the schemaVersion + BufferLayout pairing is the only thing that gives those bytes meaning.

changelog: Single-sourced 12 duplicate-carded types (StockSpec/SupplierSku/PriceQuote/PriceTier/SectionProperties/Piece → materials-stock; ToolDefinition/MeasurementGrammar/RenderMesh/MeshInstance/MaterialRef → workspace-render; PrescriptiveLookup → reference-flyweights); non-canonical copies demoted to thin cross-ref markers (S1 dry).

Opened 6 closed material-discriminator enums to registry keys (MaterialFamilyKey, ProfileKey, QuantityBasisKey, ConnectionMethodKey, UomKey) — adding a material is a leaf+row, never a core edit (S1 material-extensibility).
Removed SectionProperties.basis enum from geometry — section basis is a strategy decision behind the seam.
Enforced money invariant: 21 cents-typed fields → real decimal USD via PriceQuote (S1 base-unit money conflict).
Re-stereotyped MechanicalProperties→«reference», CutOperation→«entity», Seam→«entity», CostBenchmark→«service»; «interface» reserved for DesignStandard (S2/S3 DDD).

Normalized solver.*/architecture.*/drawing.*/designStandard.* crossRef namespace drift to delivered layerIds; renamed stale tokens (LumberStock→Stock, NDSDesignValues→MechanicalProperties, TakeoffLine→TakeoffItem, ProjectEstimate→Estimate, ConnectionEdge→Connection) (S2 label-drift).
Added project-context layer (Project/SiteCriteria) to produce the site inputs loads/estimating consume; added drawings-export terminal layer (S1 missing-stage).
openQuestions: Markup vs allowance compounding order and tax base — application sequence stated but not fully formalized (estimating S3).
19.2in OC = 614.4 ticks is not integer — store rounding policy (614/615) or a moduleRef; currently noted, not enforced (base-unit S3).
Material leaves as data «reference» vs behavior (Strategy implementers) — legend treats them as catalogs; revisit if methods need first-class modeling.
CutOperation as recorded event vs process — re-stereotyped «entity» (id-bearing, persisted); a pure-process variant may still be wanted.
